title: Renewal QBR — short-window (45m) + proxy-host / SOW checklist
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Garcia <alex.garcia@heartland.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal QBR — short-window (45m) + proxy-host / SOW checklist

Alex —

Quick note as we tighten the calendar for your Q3 renewal: the execs asked for a compact, 45-minute roadmap + QBR so we can keep it focused for leadership. Options we commonly run when schedules are tight:
- 45m live walkthrough with one of our senior AMs + product lead (recommended)
- 45m live with a recorded deep-dive sent as pre-read (works if you have time constraints)
- Proxy-host: if key stakeholders can’t make the live window, we can run the session with a delegated host and record for playback.

Can you confirm whether you prefer a live exec-focused 45m window or a recorded pre-read + 30m live Q&A this week? I’ve included a draft 45m agenda attached. Also flagged procurement and legal requirements below so we can get SOW/DocuSign queued if you’re ready to move.

Proposed windows (PST):
- Thu 17 Jul 10:00–10:45
- Thu 17 Jul 15:30–16:15
- Fri 18 Jul 08:30–09:15

If none of these work, let me know your blackout windows and we’ll propose alt slots.

Attachments: QBR_Agenda_45m_v1.pdf

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0192


From: Alex Garcia <alex.garcia@heartland.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Elena Morris <elena.morris@heartland.health>, procurement@heartland.health
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — short-window (45m) + proxy-host / SOW checklist

Thanks Ben — appreciated.

Quick constraints from my side: executive blackout between 15 Jul 14:00–17:00 for a board prep meeting, so the Thu 15:30 slot won’t work. The team prefers live but two of our VPs are overseas (UK) and prefer something that starts after 16:00 PST or early next week.

Procurement looped in (procurement@heartland.health) — they’ll need the SOW and the DP Addendum before we lock dates. Elena (CC) is our renewals lead and will coordinate the DocuSign.

Would the Tue 22 Jul 16:30–17:15 PST be possible on your side? If not, we’ll do a 45m pre-read + 30m live Q&A the week of 21 Jul.

–Alex
Director, Platform Integrations
Heartland Health

> 
> Quick note as we tighten the calendar for your Q3 renewal: the execs asked for a compact, 45-minute roadmap + QBR so we can keep it focused for leadership...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Garcia <alex.garcia@heartland.health>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 16:20:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Renewal QBR — short-window (45m) + proxy-host / SOW checklist

Ben / Alex —

We can do Tue 22 Jul 16:30–17:15 PST. I’ll be the primary presenter and Priyom will cover the technical 5–10 minute deep-dive if time allows. Operational notes:
- I’ll prepare a condensed deck that maps features to the renewal ROI bullets so execs can scan quickly.
- We should prepare a short pre-read (2 slides) we can send 24 hours prior for the UK folks.
- Ben — can you push the SOW draft to Elena and procurement today? I’ll attach a minimal SOW template and our Service Addendum.

Ben: I’m also adding a proxy-host contingency: if a VP drops, we can have a product director run the session and record. Please add that language to the runbook and call it out at kick-off.

Marissa
Customer Success Lead | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

From: procurement@heartland.health
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Garcia <alex.garcia@heartland.health>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@heartland.health>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — short-window (45m) + proxy-host / SOW checklist

Ben / Marissa —

Procurement here. Two quick items before we can route the SOW to legal:
1) We need confirmation of data residency (US only) and whether any patient PHI could be included in logs.
2) Please provide the SOW draft and your standard Data Processing Addendum (DPA).

If you can attach SOW_RenewalDraft.docx and the DPA to an email we’ll start the internal review. Assuming no major redlines, we can turn DocuSign within 3 business days.

Procurement Team
Heartland Health

> Ben / Alex —
> 
> We can do Tue 22 Jul 16:30–17:15 PST...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: procurement@heartland.health, Alex Garcia <alex.garcia@heartland.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — short-window (45m) + proxy-host / SOW checklist

Thanks — attaching the draft SOW and the minimal DPA now. Quick answers inline:
- Data residency: US only, all production logs are hosted in our US regions. We do not ingest patient PHI into indexing pipelines unless explicitly configured; default telemetry excludes patient identifiers.
- SOW attached: SOW_RenewalDraft.docx
- DPA attached: Redwood_DPA_minimal.pdf (included inside SOW bundle)

Marissa and I will mark the calendar for Tue 22 Jul 16:30–17:15 PST and send the 2-slide pre-read by Mon 21 Jul EOD. If procurement hits any legal redlines that change delivery timelines, we’ll send an updated runbook.

Attachments: SOW_RenewalDraft.docx

Ben
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0192

title: MSA & Order Form — Security exhibit: scoped inspection, pen-test staging window, and evidence queue
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Tran <paul.tran@helixhealth.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MSA & Order Form — Security exhibit: scoped inspection, pen-test staging window, and evidence queue

Hi Paul / Lena,

Thanks for the call yesterday — following up with our annotated redlines on the order form and a few open questions on the security exhibit / audit rights. Attached is a PDF with inline comments (redlines_ben_helix_orderform_20250713.pdf). High-level asks so your team can route to engineering and legal: 

- SOC 2: Can you confirm you accept a SOC 2 Type II attestation (most recent 12-month window) as the primary evidence for control assertions for production systems? If not, please indicate which control families you need sampled artifacts for.
- Pen test: We are OK with a credentialed pen test in staging. We need a firm window and agreed scope — staging only unless we explicitly agree otherwise. Proposed windows: Jul 28–30 (non-prod).
- Inspection scope & evidence cadence: We typically provide a 30-day rolling sample of logs and a 90-day snapshot on request. For inspection-lite requests, we can provide labeled artifacts and an evidence matrix; for full inspection we require additional approvals and a formal PO / inspection agreement.
- Access mechanics: 48-hour business notice for any interactive sessions, pre-signed S3 links for log bundles, and workstation-level access through our jumpbox with an RBAC session.

Proposed timeline (mutual action plan):
- 7/17: Helix legal confirms examples of inspection language they need
- 7/20: Redwood legal provides MSA redline with narrowed inspection clause
- 7/25: Redwood delivers evidence bundle for your engineering review
- 7/28–7/30: Agreed staging pen-test window (tentative)

Please review the attached redlines and the two bullets above, and let us know what you need from our side to get legal and security aligned.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Security & Trust
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: redlines_ben_helix_orderform_20250713.pdf (annotated redline)


From: Paul Tran <paul.tran@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@helixhealth.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Jul 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA & Order Form — Security exhibit: scoped inspection, pen-test staging window, and evidence queue

Ben — thanks for the clear summary and the redlines. A few items from our side: 

1) SOC 2: Helix accepts SOC 2 Type II as primary evidence for most control families. For a handful of data residency and encryption assertions we'll ask for sampled artifacts (key rotation logs, KMS policies). If you can point us to the specific report period that will be sufficient for legal.

2) Pen-test scope: Staging-only credentialed pen-test is acceptable. Our security team prefers the Jul 28–30 window you proposed. We'll plan an external vuln scan + 2-day credentialed assessment inside staging. We'll need a target list (IP ranges, service names) and a short attack brief by 7/24.

3) Evidence cadence & delivery: The evidence matrix attached (helix_evidence_matrix_draft_20250713.xlsx) lists required artifacts and preferred formats. For logs we prefer JSON line-delimited exports with a 30-day sample and a rolling 90-day retention snapshot where available. Pre-signed S3 links are fine for transfer.

4) Inspection mechanics: We prefer an "inspection lite" flow first — review of artifacts and a 2-hour technical walkthrough. If any gaps remain, we can escalate to an on-site or deeper remote inspection with a narrower scope.

I've attached the draft evidence matrix. Happy to sync with your infra folks early next week to confirm targets.

-Paul

--
Paul Tran
Director, Security Operations
Helix Health
paul.tran@helixhealth.com
+1 (617) 555-0210

Attachments: helix_evidence_matrix_draft_20250713.xlsx


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Paul Tran <paul.tran@helixhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA & Order Form — Security exhibit: scoped inspection, pen-test staging window, and evidence queue

Ben / team — reviewing Paul's asks and the attached evidence matrix. A few drafting suggestions to keep the MSA practical and narrow audit windows: 

- Narrowed inspection clause: propose language that limits inspection rights to "reasonable, pre-scheduled inspections of discrete artifacts or systems supporting the Services, conducted during Redwood business hours, and subject to 48 hours' advance written notice." This mirrors our standard practice and reduces risk of open-ended on-site inspections.

- Pen-test carve: explicitly state pen-tests require mutual scoping documentation (target list, timebox, tools allowed) and are limited to staging or non-production environments unless a separate addendum is executed. Also include a clause for blast-radius controls and rollback plan acceptance.

- Evidence substitution: allow for SOC 2 Type II plus supplemental sampled artifacts in lieu of unlimited evidence access. For encryption assertions, accept KMS rotation logs plus evidence of key policies instead of full DB dumps.

- Data transfer & retention: clarify that log bundles will be delivered via pre-signed links and retained by Helix no longer than 90 days unless an exception is negotiated.

If Laura and Legal are good with this framing I can draft exact redline language to insert into Section 9 (Inspection & Audit) and the Order Form Appendix. That draft will aim to be explicit about notice periods, scope, and technical constraints so Helix can run their checklist without opening a broader MSA re-negotiation.

Karthik

--
Karthik Iyer
Senior Counsel, Commercial Contracts
Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com

On Sun, Jul 13, 2025 at 11:05 AM Paul Tran <paul.tran@helixhealth.com> wrote:
> Ben — thanks for the clear summary and the redlines. A few items from our side: 
> 
> 1) SOC 2: Helix accepts SOC 2 Type II as primary evidence for most control families...

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA & Order Form — Security exhibit: scoped inspection, pen-test staging window, and evidence queue

Paul / Lena / team — thanks for the quick turn. Summarizing agreed items and next steps so we have a single place to track: 

- SOC 2: Helix confirmed SOC 2 Type II acceptance for most controls; Helix will flag any control families that require sampled artifacts by 7/17.
- Evidence matrix: Helix provided the draft matrix — Redwood will map our artifact names to your required fields and deliver the first evidence bundle by 7/25. (Attachment: evidence_bundle_plan_20250725.zip (stub))
- Pen-test window: Tentative staging window set for 7/28–7/30. Helix to provide attack brief and target list by 7/24. Redwood infra will publish IP ranges and jumpbox connection instructions by 7/25.
- MSA language: Karthik will circulate proposed redline language for Section 9 and the Order Form Appendix by 7/20 for Helix legal review.

Next steps (action owners):
- Paul / Helix security: confirm any additional control families that need sampled artifacts (due 7/17).
- Karthik: draft MSA redline (due 7/20).
- Ben / Infra: produce evidence bundle (due 7/25) and staging connection details (7/25).
- Both teams: sync 7/24 for pen-test attack brief and final window confirmation.

If that looks accurate I can convert this into a mutual action plan doc and drop into the HubSpot deal record. Appreciate the collaborative approach.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter

Attachments: evidence_bundle_plan_20250725.zip (outline, will include logs & KMS extracts)

title: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Neha — looping you in for an infra leadership review.

We’re finalizing semantics for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max). A recurring enterprise ask is “set min high enough to guarantee baseline throughput,” but we need a clear stance on what happens when region capacity can’t meet a customer’s min.

Questions we need to lock this week:
1) If a customer sets min to a level we can’t provision in-region (shortage), what behavior + comms do we want? (degraded state, alerts, CS guidance)
2) Should we enforce any guardrails at config time (e.g., recommended min range, approvals for very high mins, per-account caps)?
3) Capacity planning implications: do we need a new internal workflow to reserve headroom when mins increase?

Vanessa has a proposal draft for the autoscaler behavior. I attached a 1-pager with shortage scenarios.

Attachment: min-bounds-shortage-scenarios-v2.pdf (application/pdf)

Related: SUP-28452 (min not met during region shortage), ENG-22151 (shortage behavior).

Can you take a look and reply with your concerns / suggested policy? If helpful, we can do a 20 min sync today.

Thanks,
Lauren
Product Lead, Dedicated


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Thanks — read the 1-pager.

My main concern is that “min” will be interpreted contractually (even if we don’t intend it that way) and will cause escalations when regional supply is constrained. If we ship this without crisp semantics + surfacing, we’ll create a failure mode that looks like: customer sets min, Redwood “accepts” it, later we can’t fulfill, and the autoscaler thrashes / keeps trying.

What I’d like to see:
- Explicit degraded state when min cannot be met (and it should be sticky + rate-limited to avoid flapping).
- Clear separation between:
  a) configured min (customer intent)
  b) effective provisioned capacity (what we could actually allocate)
  c) reason codes (region shortage vs quota vs internal error).
- Comms + operational flow:
  - automatic alert to on-call + CS when a customer is below min for >N minutes
  - customer-facing message in Console (“We are currently unable to allocate the configured minimum due to regional capacity constraints; contact support”)
  - no silent fallback that hides the fact we’re violating the min intent.

On guardrails:
- I’m in favor of a “soft” warning for high mins and a “hard” admin approval for extremely high mins in a single region (i.e., values that materially affect fleet headroom). This can be internal-only at first.

Capacity planning:
- We should treat changes to min as a capacity event and pipe them into our planning dashboard. Otherwise, we’ll get surprised by multiple customers ratcheting mins.

Vanessa: can you confirm whether the current implementation will keep attempting scale-up to min every loop? If so, we need backoff to prevent thundering herd behavior during shortage.

Priya — would like your take on whether we want to promise anything around “min” in GA language, or make it clear it’s a best-effort control with shortage signaling.

-Neha
Infra / Reliability


---

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 10:31 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Confirming behavior + proposal.

Current autoscaler loop (pre-shortage work):
- Computes desired replicas from reactive signals (queue depth/saturation) + optional predictive bias
- Clamps desired into [min, max]
- Submits to fleet allocator

If the allocator can’t place GPUs (regional shortage), today it returns “insufficient capacity,” and we retry on the next loop. Without additional logic, yes, we’d keep trying each cycle.

What we’re planning under ENG-22151 / PR-48270:
- Introduce a distinct state when provisioned < min due to allocator shortage:
  - emit metric: dedicated_autoscaler_min_unmet{deployment_id, region, reason="insufficient_capacity"}
  - add exponential backoff on allocator retries (with a cap), to avoid stampeding the allocator
  - keep desired clamped to min (intent preserved) but prevent rapid repeated place attempts
- Surface “min unmet” in structured logs and the capacity signal feed to routing so routing can degrade gracefully.

Open question we need product/leadership decision on:
- Do we block saving config with very high min values unless fleet has headroom? That’s hard because headroom is dynamic. Alternative is to allow save but show warning + make “min unmet” explicit.

Also: the “min unmet” condition should not trigger scale-down logic; it should freeze scale-down decisions while unmet (we don’t want oscillation).

Happy to jump on a call.

-Vanessa
Fleet Autoscaling


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 11:18 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Agree with Neha’s framing: we need deterministic semantics + explicit signaling.

Decision direction for GA:
1) “Min” is a hard bound on autoscaler intent (we will not intentionally scale below it), but it is not a guarantee of instantaneous provisioning in a capacity shortage.
2) We must have first-class “min unmet” degraded state in Console and in alerts.
3) We should not block config saves based on current headroom (too volatile), but:
   - add warnings for high mins
   - add internal approval gate (behind an admin workflow) for unusually large min increases on a single deployment/region during pilot/early GA.
4) Capacity planning: log all min changes as events and get a weekly rollup of total committed mins per region (by tier) so infra planning is not blind.

Lauren: please ensure the release notes/docs do not imply “min == reserved capacity” unless it’s explicitly coupled with a contract SKU.

Vanessa: ensure allocator retry backoff and state-stickiness are in the canary criteria.

-Priya
Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 12:07 PM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

+1.

Two additional operational asks:
- Alerting thresholds should differentiate:
  a) min unmet due to shortage (page only if sustained + customer impact metrics show queue/latency)
  b) min unmet due to internal errors (page faster)

- CS comms template: “We are seeing regional capacity constraints; here are options” (temporary max increase in alternate region is out-of-scope now, but at least we can suggest raising max in-region if the issue is not shortage, or reducing model concurrency, etc.).

Lauren: can we ensure SUP-28452’s customer story is reflected in the CS playbook? This is exactly the failure mode that becomes a fire drill.

Vanessa: please include reason codes we can use in paging + dashboards (insufficient_capacity vs quota vs allocator_error).

-Neha


---

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 2:38 PM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

This is super helpful — thanks all.

Captured decisions / actions:
- Product semantics: min is hard intent bound; shortages -> explicit “min unmet” degraded state (no implied guarantee of provisioning).
- Eng (Vanessa): implement allocator backoff + sticky degraded state + reason codes; ensure signal is exported to routing.
- SRE/Obs: add alerting split by reason; wire into ENG-22145 scope.
- Docs/Release: avoid language that equates min to “reserved” unless paired with contract; add FAQ: “What happens if min cannot be met?”
- Capacity planning: we’ll create an event stream for min changes; weekly regional rollup to infra planning (pilot first).
- Guardrails: Console warning for high mins; internal approval gate (admin workflow) for unusually large min increases during pilot/early GA.

I’ll update the PRD + ADR notes and link to ENG-22151. Also will make sure SUP-28452 is incorporated into the CS escalation playbook.

If anything else comes up as you review PR-48270 / the canary plan, please flag.

-Lauren

- Review architecture and data flows (20–30 min)

title: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Park <olivia@semaphorelabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Hi Olivia,

Thanks again for the demo last week — loved how you framed the latency requirements for the mobile use case. Per our conversation, I wanted to put a concise proposal on the table for a seed-stage onboarding package that balances runway protection for Semaphore Labs and gives you room to iterate without immediate cost pressure.

High-level offer (proposal A):
- Onboarding subsidy: $5,000 in platform credits redeemable over the first 6 months
- Early-stage usage rebate: 20% rebate on usage billed in the first 12 months (rebate applied as a credit on monthly invoices) up to $50k billed
- Technical onboarding included (2x 2-hour sessions + priority queue for the first 30 days)
- Standard hosted terms; we can add a Marketplace or Dedicated path later if you scale

Attachments: draft-sla-and-sponsor-addendum.pdf (draft addendum to include subsidy and rebate mechanics)

If that looks reasonable I can: 1) loop in Finance to confirm invoicing flow, and 2) send a DocuSign addendum for a straightforward approval path. If you'd prefer more upfront credits in lieu of the rebate we can trade there — happy to discuss.

Are you available Wednesday for a 30-minute call to finalize?

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
---
Alex Martinez | +1 (415) 555-0142 | alex@redwood.com

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 12:28:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Looping you in — Olivia at Semaphore wants more upfront credits (8k) or a clearer tiered rebate. They have a procurement review Tuesday. Can we decide on an approved option I can send today? Below I sketched three options to present (compact tradeoffs so procurement can pick quickly):

Option 1 — Runway-first
- $8,000 onboarding credit (6 months)
- No additional rebate
- Standard 30-day net invoicing
- Requires procurement sign-off only

Option 2 — Hybrid (preferred by me)
- $4,000 onboarding credit
- Tiered rebate: 25% rebate on monthly invoice dollars up to $25k, 15% rebate between $25k–$50k, cap at $50k (rebates applied monthly as invoice credits)
- Adds one line in addendum about how we calculate spike months (7-day rolling average to prevent gaming)

Option 3 — Rebate-heavy
- $2,500 onboarding credit
- Flat 30% rebate on first $40k billed
- Requires Finance pre-approval for rebate accounting flow

Questions for the group:
- Ben: which option will most likely close fast from a commercial POV?
- Naomi: any accounting or rev-rec issues we need to flag (rebates as contra-revenue vs credit)?
- Irene: legal redlines to the draft addendum I attached — anything that would add a >7 day review cycle?

Attachments: capability-overview.pdf (they asked for the deck section), draft-sla-and-sponsor-addendum.pdf

Thanks —
Alex

---
Ben Carter wrote:
> Ship it. Option 2 is a clean commercial compromise — shows flexibility but keeps us aligned to usage economics.

Naomi Feldman wrote:
> Rebate mechanics need to be explicit in the SOW for rev-rec. I can sign off on tiered if Finance confirms the credit application workflow (apply to next invoice, track as deferred credit).

Irene Choi wrote:
> I can turn the addendum in 48 hours if we stick to standard language for subsidy and rebate mechanics. Anything custom for data residency or indemnities will add time.


From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Park <olivia@semaphorelabs.com>
Cc: procurement@semaphorelabs.com, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 01:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Hi Olivia — thanks for the quick feedback and for flagging procurement's timeline. We discussed internally and can make a near-term offer that balances your runway needs and our usage alignment.

Final offer (compromise, Option B variant):
- Onboarding subsidy: $5,000 platform credits (redeemable over first 6 months)
- Tiered rebate: 22% rebate on monthly invoice dollars up to $30k; 12% rebate on the portion between $30k–$60k; rebates applied as credits on the following invoice; rebate cap at $60k for the first 12 months
- Technical onboarding: 2x 2-hour working sessions + two weeks of priority support for incident triage
- Invoicing: Net 30, rebates applied as credits; accounting will track credits as deferred liabilities per standard practice
- Legal: simple addendum only; no custom indemnities or data-residency clauses in this package (we can engage for Private deployment later)

Attachments: addendum-semaphore-draft.docx (DocuSign-ready; includes rebate mechanics and measurement approach — we use a 7-day rolling average to smooth spike months)

Next steps:
1) I can send the DocuSign addendum now for procurement approval.
2) If sign-off is quick, we'll schedule the onboarding session within 7 business days of execution.

If your procurement team prefers the higher upfront credit instead of tiered rebate, we can swap to $7.5k upfront and remove the rebate — let me know which path you prefer.

Happy to join a short call if you want to walk through the invoice/rebate examples.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

  • Wed Jul 16, 14:00–14:20 PT

title: EU/UK SCCs — module confirmation and signing logistics for MedAlign DPA
preview:
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@medalign.com>
To: ben.carter@redwood.com
Cc: legal@medalign.com
Date: Thu, 10 Jul 2025 14:12:00 +0100
Subject: SCCs module preference + signing flow for MedAlign DPA

Hi Ben,

Hope you’re well — we’re finalising the DPA for the MedAlign <> Redwood engagement and need to confirm two things before procurement can route to signature:

1) SCCs module selection for EU -> US processing: based on our controller role and the planned data flows, do you recommend a specific module (Controller->Processor vs Controller->Controller variants)? If possible we’d like a brief reason so our legal team can close the loop.

2) Execution/signing logistics: are you set up for DocuSign execution? If so, who should sign on Redwood’s side (company name and title) and do you have any signing order preference?

We also need examples of executed SCCs (redacted is fine), and a list of subprocessors for our records. I’ve attached a short questionnaire with our ask.

Appreciate a quick confirmation — procurement is aiming to complete the contract by next week.

Thanks,
Samantha Lee
Senior Counsel | MedAlign Health
Attachments: MedAlign_SCCs_Query.docx


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@medalign.com>
Cc: laura.bennett@redwood.com, vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com
Date: Thu, 10 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: SCCs module preference + signing flow for MedAlign DPA

Hi Samantha — thanks for the clear note. Quick answers below and next steps.

1) Module recommendation: For the MedAlign use-case (you as Controller, Redwood as Processor), we recommend the Controller -> Processor path (commonly referenced as Module Two in the SCCs set) plus the UK Addendum for transfers originating from the UK. Reason: it maps to the roles in your data flows and avoids unilateral controller-to-controller assumptions.

2) Signing: we can execute via DocuSign. Redwood will sign as the Processor; our signature block will be by an authorized signatory in Legal. If you’d like Redwood to initiate the envelope we can do that once you confirm the final DPA PDF. Otherwise, feel free to send the DocuSign envelope and we’ll countersign.

Requested evidence: we can provide a redacted executed SCCs example and a current subprocessor roster. I’ve looped in Laura (Legal) and Vivek (Security/Product) to own those artifacts and timing.

Proposed timeline:
- Subprocessor roster: deliver within 48 hours
- Redacted example SCCs + signed template for DocuSign: within 5 business days

If that works I’ll coordinate delivery and provide the DocuSign link when the DPA is final.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference

To: ben.carter@redwood.com
Cc: legal@medalign.com
Date: Fri, 11 Jul 2025 09:18:00 +0100
Subject: Re: SCCs module preference + signing flow for MedAlign DPA

Thanks Ben — that roadmap sounds reasonable. A couple of follow-ups from our side:

- For the subprocessor roster, we need the current list with processing purpose and hosting location (country).
- For the redacted SCCs example, could you include the signature page and the executed date range (redacted company names ok)? That helps our audit trail.
- Are you able to accept a limited number of minor DPA redlines (we usually ask for a small change to breach notification timing and an extra clause around security testing windows)? If Redwood needs to remain on the template you provided, please state that explicitly.

I’ve attached a short doc that outlines the data categories and some questions your security team can answer directly.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — procurement is the bottleneck.

Best,
Samantha

title: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Wallace <ethan_wallace@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Noura El-Khoury <noura_el-khoury@redwoodinference.com>, Maya Chen <maya@northpeakpr.com>
Date: Wed, Jul 9, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)

Team — we’re in the last mile. Can we do a final pass today on:

1) Press release (quotes + any claims language)
2) Press kit ZIP contents + links (landing page, PDF, methodology)
3) Analyst/press email template (Maya)

Target: lock by 2:00pm PT so Northpeak can queue distribution for tomorrow 8:00am PT.

Current links (please confirm these are final):
- Landing page: https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar
- PDF report: https://redwoodinference.com/reports/latency-per-dollar-2025q3.pdf
- Methodology: https://redwoodinference.com/methodology/latency-per-dollar

Open items I’m tracking:
- Jordan quote: please confirm final wording.
- Legal: confirm whether we can say “best-in-class” anywhere (I believe no).
- Press kit: is the 1-pager methodology summary included (for folks who won’t read the full PDF)?

Attaching:
- RedwoodInference_BenchmarkReport_PressRelease_v7.docx
- RedwoodInference_PressKit_2025-07-10.zip
- Benchmark_Methodology_Summary_1pager.pdf

Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


---

From: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Wallace <ethan_wallace@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Maya Chen <maya@northpeakpr.com>
Date: Wed, Jul 9, 2025 at 9:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)

All — I reviewed v7 and have two last-minute edits that I think we should make before Legal re-checks.

Edits requested (low-risk):
1) Replace “best-in-class latency-per-dollar” with “leading latency-per-dollar” (or “strong latency-per-dollar”) in the lead.
   - Current: “best-in-class latency-per-dollar across regions”
   - Proposed: “high latency-per-dollar across regions”

2) In the “How we measured” paragraph, add the explicit caveat that results vary by workload and configuration, with a pointer to methodology.
   - Proposed sentence: “Results vary by workload and configuration; full methodology and run artifacts are available at …”

Press kit ZIP contents check:
- ✅ Press release (doc)
- ✅ Report PDF
- ✅ PNG chart set (3)
- ✅ Company boilerplate
- ✅ Logos (approved)
- ✅ Methodology 1-pager (included)

Ben — can you confirm the PDF URL is stable (no redirects tomorrow morning)?

Soojin
Director of Product Marketing


---

From: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Wallace <ethan_wallace@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Maya Chen <maya@northpeakpr.com>
Date: Wed, Jul 9, 2025 at 9:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)

Legal notes on v7 (quick):

1) Please remove “best-in-class” and “#1” type language anywhere it appears.
   - “Leading” is also borderline depending on context. Safer: “measured latency-per-dollar” / “production-focused latency-per-dollar benchmarks” / “optimized for latency and cost efficiency.”

2) Any competitor reference needs to be either:
   a) purely contextual (no ranking, no absolute claim), AND
   b) paired with a “results vary” caveat + link to methodology.

3) Quotes: fine to express product philosophy and what the report covers, but avoid “proves we are fastest/cheapest.”

If Soojin’s edits are incorporated, we can do a final skim within the hour.

Michael Grant
Legal, Redwood Inference


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>

Jordan Blake, Chief Revenue Officer, Redwood Inference

Jordan

To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>

Date: Tue, 1 Jul 2025 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: High latency during nightly batch - Peregrine

Hi Camila,

We saw a large latency regression on our nightly embedding batch that started at ~02:05 UTC and lasted about 1.5 hours. Average per-request p50 jumped from ~120ms to ~1.8s and several jobs timed out. This impacted our nightly model refresh and blocked a handful of downstream jobs.

Can you confirm if this was on your side and whether you can provide any credits for the impact? Our renewal is coming up and this incident materially affected our testing window.

I've attached a small excerpt of logs and job IDs. Happy to jump on a short call.

Thanks,
Liam O'Connell

Thanks for the ping. Quick notes from SRE triage:
- Root cause: transient autoscaler bug in the routing layer that delayed kernel warmup on a small subset of nodes.
- Impact window: 2025-07-01T02:05:00Z to 2025-07-01T03:35:00Z (approximately 90 minutes).
- Affected model variant: embed-large-v2-quant (customer pinned).
- Observed symptom: increased first-token latency and occasional connection timeouts for long batches.

Actions taken: we rolled a hotfix at 03:40Z and proactively rerouted traffic; we also warmed affected nodes. We have a postmortem draft attached.

Preliminary estimate for customer impact (per our standard credit guidance): for a 90-minute full-service degradation affecting ~40% of customer requests, recommendation is a goodwill credit equivalent to 20% of that day's bill for impacted usage or an equivalent compute credit. I'm attaching a short credit_worksheet.csv with calculations.

Marcus

title: Default capture horizons and deletion steps — prospect Q&A
preview:
From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@finledger.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@finledger.com
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick Q: default data capture windows and deletion steps for POC

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week. Before we start the POC we need to confirm a few retention/residency details so our security team can sign off. Can you confirm the default handling for the following scenarios on Redwood hosted API (no Dedicated yet):

- Interactive chat (single user sessions) — how long are the raw request and the model response kept by default?
- Streaming transcripts (audio->tokens) — are intermediate tokens stored, and for how long?
- KV cache / session warm caches — do you persist these between requests and for how long?
- Embeddings (batch/async indexing) — default retention and who can access them?
- File attachments uploaded to a request (PDFs we supply) — retention and purge flow?

Also:
- Can we constrain storage to EU regions for EU customer data (we're running Lisbon and Frankfurt infra)?
- For erasure requests from a user or regulator, what evidence and timeline do you provide?

If you have a short one-pager or a sample DPA clause we can review, that would be ideal.

Thanks,
Amelia
Amelia Torres
Head of Security, FinLedger
amelia.torres@finledger.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@finledger.com>
Cc: security@finledger.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick Q: default data capture windows and deletion steps for POC

Amelia — great questions, thanks. Below is a concise mapping of our default behavior for hosted Redwood API (we can change anything via Dedicated/Private or per-customer config during the POC):

1) Interactive requests and final outputs
- Default retention: 45 days. This covers the request payload (prompts) and the final model response that we store for observability, debugging, and quality monitoring.
- Access: scoped to your org in the console; individual engineering/admin roles plus our minimal SRE escalation paths (audited).

2) Streaming tokens / partial transcripts
- Default behavior: transient buffering. Partial tokens are used to assemble the response but are purged from the short-lived buffer within 24 hours by default. They are not indexed as separate records unless you opt into session transcripts.

3) KV cache / session warm caches
- Default: ephemeral persistence to improve latency; caches are maintained for up to 7 days to allow quick session recovery and autoscaling warm-ups. If you require zero warm-cache persistence, we can set retention.policy=NO_CACHE for the tenant.

4) Embeddings and indexing artifacts
- Default retention: 365 days. Embeddings are treated as indexable artifacts and retained to support search/semantic features. You can request shorter retention or immediate deletion per-batch.

5) Uploaded attachments (PDFs, docs)
- Default retention: 90 days in object storage tied to the request record. Attachments are stored encrypted at rest; deletion of the parent request or an erasure request will schedule attachment purge as well.

6) Residency
- We support region pinning for hosted accounts. For EU residency we persist artifacts in our EU regions (Frankfurt and Paris/Amsterdam depending on availability) — choose region=eu at account creation.

7) Erasure / evidence
- Timeline: standard erasure requests are executed within 10 business days.
- Evidence: we provide an auditable attestation and a time-stamped audit snippet showing data deletion events (object keys removed, DB tombstones applied). For escalations we can provide a signed attestation.

How to opt-out or change defaults
- Console: Console -> Security -> Data Residency & Retention to set org-wide defaults (retention windows, region pin).
- API/Request level: include header X-Redwood-Retention: no-store to request non-persistence for that call (useful for PII-heavy interactions).
- For programmatic purge we expose redwoodctl (CLI) in Dedicated/Private: redwoodctl purge --project <id> --since <iso-date> --types requests,attachments.

I've attached a short summary (retention-summary-stub.pdf) and a screenshot of the retention panel for reference. Looping in Kimberly for the erasure attestation language.

—Ben

Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Amelia — great questions, thanks. Below is a concise mapping of our default behavior for hosted Redwood API (we can change anything via Dedicated/Private or per-customer config during the POC):

1) Interactive requests and final outputs
- Default retention: 45 days. This covers the request payload (prompts) and the final model response that we store for observability, debugging, and quality monitoring.
- Access: scoped to your org in the console; individual engineering/admin roles plus our minimal SRE escalation paths (audited).

2) Streaming tokens / partial transcripts
- Default behavior: transient buffering. Partial tokens are used to assemble the response but are purged from the short-lived buffer within 24 hours by default. They are not indexed as separate records unless you opt into session transcripts.

3) KV cache / session warm caches
- Default: ephemeral persistence to improve latency; caches are maintained for up to 7 days to allow quick session recovery and autoscaling warm-ups. If you require zero warm-cache persistence, we can set retention.policy=NO_CACHE for the tenant.

4) Embeddings and indexing artifacts
- Default retention: 365 days. Embeddings are treated as indexable artifacts and retained to support search/semantic features. You can request shorter retention or immediate deletion per-batch.

5) Uploaded attachments (PDFs, docs)
- Default retention: 90 days in object storage tied to the request record. Attachments are stored encrypted at rest; deletion of the parent request or an erasure request will schedule attachment purge as well.

6) Residency
- We support region pinning for hosted accounts. For EU residency we persist artifacts in our EU regions (Frankfurt and Paris/Amsterdam depending on availability) — choose region=eu at account creation.

7) Erasure / evidence
- Timeline: standard erasure requests are executed within 10 business days.
- Evidence: we provide an auditable attestation and a time-stamped audit snippet showing data deletion events (object keys removed, DB tombstones applied). For escalations we can provide a signed attestation.

How to opt-out or change defaults
- Console: Console -> Security -> Data Residency & Retention to set org-wide defaults (retention windows, region pin).
- API/Request level: include header X-Redwood-Retention: no-store to request non-persistence for that call (useful for PII-heavy interactions).
- For programmatic purge we expose redwoodctl (CLI) in Dedicated/Private: redwoodctl purge --project <id> --since <iso-date> --types requests,attachments.

title: Phased rollout: usage safety net & overage terms
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Stevens <j.stevens@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-29T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Phased rollout: usage safety net & overage terms

Jordan —

Great chatting earlier. As we scope the MAP and the evaluation plan, ACME's staged rollout poses a few commercial questions our finance and delivery teams need covered before we finalize timelines:

1) Safety net during evaluation: can we agree on a soft-cap with automatic alerts during the first 6 weeks of ramp and a clearly priced temporary burst option if you exceed it?

2) Overage vs. emergency burst: we typically provide a two-tier approach — a 'soft cap' with no hard enforcement (alerts + throttling suggestions) and an 'emergency burst' (manual approval, higher per-token rate). We'd like to put those exact numbers in the MAP so procurement can sign.

3) Rate card snapshot: we'll attach the June ratecard and a draft MAP that recommends staged commits tied to milestones (PO, staging success, 1M usable tokens). Please call out any target ceilings that are non-negotiable for ACME.

Attached: proposed_MAP_v2.pdf, ratecard_snapshot_Jun25.xlsx, evaluation_schedule_v1.xlsx

High level ask: can you confirm by EOD Thursday which safety-net level ACME is comfortable with (soft cap at 50k tokens/day with alerts / hard cap at 200k tokens/day / emergency burst up to 500k at elevated rate)? Once we have that, I can escalate for a short-term credit if you need it for the pilot.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: proposed_MAP_v2.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: ratecard_snapshot_Jun25.xlsx (Excel)]


From: Jordan Stevens <j.stevens@acme.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya.rao@acme.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-30T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phased rollout: usage safety net & overage terms

Ben —

Thanks for the summary and the docs. Quick answers from our side:

- We absolutely need a safety net while the feature is in pilot. Our procurement team wants a hard ceiling for automated enforcement during the first 30 days, with the option to request temporary increases.
- For the evaluation, can we do: hard cap = 250k tokens/day, soft alert at 60% utilization, and emergency burst up to 600k tokens/day (requires manual approval and we accept a higher overage rate)?
- We will need the exact overage rate to be called out in the MAP (per-token delta) so it clears procurement. Can you propose the delta vs standard rate?

Also looping in Priya (ops) — Priya, any SSO or network whitelist items we should flag for Redwood's private deployment during the test?

Jordan
Jordan Stevens
Director, AI Integrations
ACME Enterprises


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Stevens <j.stevens@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-30T15:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phased rollout: usage safety net & overage terms

Jordan —

Appreciate the clarity. We can agree to the hard cap and soft-alert structure you outlined. Proposed commercial guardrails:

Subject: Initial: PayGate enterprise onboarding - access controls

Hi Chloe —

Thanks for the quick intro call earlier. As promised, we want to accelerate the access-controls portion of the onboarding so our SSO cutover and service-account provisioning don't block the rest of the rollout. High-level asks from our side:

- Confirm Redwood's SAML metadata and cert rotation cadence
- SCIM provisioning mapping for groups -> roles (we have 5 admin groups to map)
- IP allowlist requirements for your control plane and runtime endpoints
- Guidance on ephemeral service accounts and long-lived machine identities for backend jobs

I'm attaching a spreadsheet with our existing groups and current SSO provider details. Can you put together a short runbook and an ETA for a dry-run?

Cheers,
Alex
IT Program Manager, PayGate

Attachments: paygate_groups_v0.csv (referenced), internal_notes.txt (internal)


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Alex Rivera <alex.rivera@paygate.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial: PayGate enterprise onboarding - access controls

Thanks Alex — great summary and thanks for the attachment. Looping in Monica (CS lead) for sequencing and Ben (implementation engineer) for technical details.

Planned next steps:
1) Ben will draft a concise access-controls runbook (SAML/SCIM/IP/service-accounts) by EOD Wed.
2) Monica will schedule a 60-min dry-run with your SSO and infra teams the week of Jul 7.
3) We'll share a small test SP metadata for PayGate to point at for validation.

For transparency, our runbook will include: SAML metadata & cert rotation notes, SCIM attribute mappings (username, email, groups), recommended IP ranges and health-check endpoints, and a pattern for ephemeral service accounts + RBAC mapping.

Ben — can you confirm whether the runtime endpoints will require static IPs from our side or if the control plane is reachable via standard outbound?

Thanks,
Chloe
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood_access_checklist_v1.xlsx (draft checklist)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Alex Rivera <alex.rivera@paygate.com>, Priya Kapoor <pri.kapoor@paygate.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial: PayGate enterprise onboarding - access controls

On Tue, Jun 30, 2025 at 10:03 AM Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com> wrote:
> Ben — can you confirm whether the runtime endpoints will require static IPs from our side or if the control plane is reachable via standard outbound?

Quick technical note from infra:
- Control plane endpoints (api.redwood-inference.com) are public and reachable over standard outbound egress; no static source IPs required for basic API calls.
- For customers who need enforcement via IP allowlist, we support a small set of egress IP ranges per region; for US region the recommended source IPs to whitelist on PayGate firewall are: 34.198.22.0/24 and 34.198.23.0/24.
- SAML: we accept either POST or REDIRECT bindings; we'll send SP metadata (saml-metadata-example.xml) for your IdP to ingest. Cert rotation: we rotate certs every 90 days; if you need a longer overlap window we can publish a 30-day overlap cert during cutover.
- SCIM: supports group push and user push; recommended mapping file: group.name -> role.name, user.email -> principalId. We'll include sample SCIM payloads in the runbook.

I'll attach the SAML metadata and a short SCIM example payload for your IdP team. Suggest the dry-run include:
- Ingest SP metadata into PayGate IdP
- Execute a SCIM create user + group push test
- Validate token exchange and a simple API call via a service account to confirm RBAC mapping

Ben
Implementation Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: saml-metadata-example.xml, scim-example-payload.json (inline sample)


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>

Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial: PayGate enterprise onboarding - access controls

Alex / Priya — thanks. I've scheduled the dry-run for Tue Jul 8 at 11:00 AM PT and invited PayGate SSO and infra. Agenda (45–60 mins):

1) Quick intros & goals (5 min)
2) SAML SP metadata ingestion + cert validation (10 min)
3) SCIM group push and user sync mapping (15 min)
4) IP allowlist verification + control-plane access test (10 min)
5) Service-account provisioning pattern and RBAC verification (10 min)

Action items before the call:
- PayGate: share IdP admin access or a point person who can ingest SP metadata (Priya confirmed).
- PayGate: provide a non-production tenant or test user list for SCIM (Alex to provide).
- Redwood: Ben to publish the small-run checklist and sample payloads to the shared Drive.

Jonas — can you review the runbook's org-policy section to ensure we capture audit/logging expectations (retention, admin audit logs) for PayGate's compliance team?

Thanks,
Monica
Customer Success Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Alex Rivera <alex.rivera@paygate.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 14:05:00 -07:00

I've reviewed the draft checklist and added the org-policy items we typically cover for enterprise customers:

- Audit logs: retention window options (30/90/365 days) and how to export logs to customer SIEM (S3/Cloud PubSub).
- Key management: KMS integrations supported (AWS KMS, GCP KMS); note that for Private deployments we'll provide HSM/HSM-backed options.
- Admin roles: recommended least-privilege role set (Owner, Admin, Observer, Support).
- Service-account lifecycle: recommended TTL for ephemeral tokens (1h) and guidance for rotating long-lived keys used by batch jobs.

I also added a short troubleshooting checklist for common SAML errors (NameID format, clock skew, cert mismatch) to speed the dry-run.

Looking forward to the call — we'll publish the final runbook to the Drive link Monica referenced.

Jonas
Security & Compliance Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: Private deployment observability: network & SIEM handoff
preview:
From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 15:12:09 -0400
Subject: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Hi Aditya / Kim,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We kicked off a security review and the team needs a concrete list of network egress paths and an approach for shipping logs/traces from the private deployment into our Splunk tenant. Specific asks from our side:

- Minimum outbound ports/IPs required (hec/syslog/s3/kafka endpoints) and whether connections are client-initiated or need persistent tunnels.
- If mutual TLS is required for HEC; cert rotation expectations and supported CA options.
- A short-runbook for incident evidence transfer (how we request packetized evidence, transport, retention).
- Any on-host agents vs. forwarder preference for private on-prem installs.

We attached our internal compliance checklist. Can you confirm what you need from our infra team to run the onramp? Also please include sample firewall rules and a sample allowlist.

Pat
Ops Lead, Acme Logistics

Attachment: acme-compliance-checklist.docx (docx)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 17:05:44 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — thanks for the clear list. I pulled together a concise network + observability handoff that should map to the checklist. High level answers first, then a proposed runbook and attachments.

High level:
- Outbound connections are client-initiated from the private control plane to our ingest endpoints. No inbound firewall holes are required into customer networks.
- Supported transports: HTTPS HEC (Splunk), TLS-wrapped syslog over TCP, S3-compatible object push for batch evidence, and Kafka (TLS) where customers already host a broker.
- HEC: mutual TLS is supported and recommended for higher assurance; we also support token-based HEC over TLS if MTLS is operationally difficult. Cert rotation: we recommend 90-day rotation with automated renewal via your internal CA or HashiVault — we document the API hooks in the attached checklist.
- Forwarder pattern: preferred model is a lightweight forwarder (constrained container) that performs local buffering, checksum bundling and signs evidence packets before pushing to Redwood-controlled ingest endpoints. This minimizes required changes to customer SIEM.

Proposed firewall allowlist (egress only) — minimal set (sample):
- 55.116.210.10/32 TCP 443 (redwood-hec-prod-1.redwood.com) — Splunk HEC endpoint for tenant ingest
- 52.18.34.0/24 TCP 443 (redwood-metrics-prod.redwood.com) — metrics push endpoint (Prometheus remote write over TLS)
- 34.201.12.87/32 TCP 514 (syslog-tls.redwood.com) — TCP/TLS syslog aggregator (optional)
- 13.59.23.128/32 TCP 443 (s3-redwood-evidence.us-west-2.amazonaws.com) — S3-compatible evidence bucket interface
- NTP outbound UDP 123 to your internal NTP or allowed pool (time drift impacts signed evidence)

Notes: we provide static egress IPs for Dedicated/private contracts; if you're using ephemeral NAT IPs we recommend setting up a small NAT pool and letting us know the range so we can update ACLs.

Runbook (short):
1) Pre-onramp: exchange tenant IDs, public keys for MTLS, and preferred ingest mode (HEC vs S3).
2) Networking: customer provisions egress ACLs for the allowlist (sample attached).
3) Deployment: we supply a forwarder container image and Helm chart (on-prem) or systemd unit (bare metal).
4) Acceptance: push a signed evidence packet and verify checksums; complete basic trace correlation sample (span id preserved).
5) Production: enable continuous metrics remote-write, set retention windows, and onboard into your Splunk index with provided field mappings.

Attachments: redwood-network-nexus-diagram-v2.pdf — includes sample topology with peering/VPN variants; siem-handshake-checklist.xlsx — mapping of checklist items to delivery owners; egress-allowlist.txt — copy-pasteable ACLs.

I'll loop in Jordan for the trace hook specifics and Marissa for the acceptance criteria. If you want, we can schedule a 30-min run-through with your infra team tomorrow afternoon.

— Aditya
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 09:48:22 -0400
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Aditya — this looks great. A few clarifications from our security team below:

- For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)? We will not share private keys; we can publish an intermediate CA bundle.
- Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json that includes UTC timestamps, sha256 checksums for each log/trace file, and the forwarder signature. Do you provide tooling to validate signatures client-side?
- Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs and preservation of trace/span metadata when exported to Splunk; our APM team will validate with a small trace run.

Also, Splunk onboarding asked whether you prefer to map logs into a neutral JSON schema prior to HEC ingest or to rely on field extraction rules inside Splunk. Their concern is consistent parsing across environments.

Can we also get the brief runbook inserted into our internal ticket system (I've attached the ticket) so our network team can pick it up.

Pat

Attachment: ACME-REQ-3987-ticket.pdf (pdf)


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 09:03:11 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — quick follow-ups and commitments from our side: 

1) MTLS: yes — we can accept a customer-supplied intermediate CA bundle. Our forwarder validates server certs and presents a client cert signed by the customer CA. We will never request private keys. Rotation: we provide hooks for automated replacement; we can also accept short-lived certs issued from your vault (HashiCorp/Enterprise PKI).

2) Evidence packet: we will provide a reference validator (small Go binary + scripts) that checks manifest.json, verifies sha256 checksums and validates the forwarder signature (ED25519). I attached a sample manifest in the design PDF and included a minimal packet example in the archive on the Drive link.

3) Tracing: our tracing hooks preserve 128-bit trace IDs and inject a redwood.trace_id field when forwarding spans to Splunk. Splunk team — we recommend pre-normalizing into a canonical JSON envelope at the forwarder; that reduces field-extraction variance. If you prefer to handle extraction in Splunk, we can ship field mapping docs instead.

4) Runbook insertion: Ack — Aditya will push the runbook into your ticketing system; we can do a quick handoff call to walk the network engineers through the ACLs.

Next steps:
- Aditya: share the allowlist file and the Helm chart link (I pushed the chart to the Drive folder).
- Kimberly: start a compliance evidence packet template that matches your retention requirements and legal hold controls.
- Marissa: I'll coordinate an integration test window (we propose Jul 10 22:00-23:30 UTC for an acceptance run).

Thanks — Jordan
Staff Software Engineer, Observability

text:
From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 15:12:09 -0400
Subject: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Hi Aditya / Kim,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We kicked off a security review and the team needs a concrete list of network egress paths and an approach for shipping logs/traces from the private deployment into our Splunk tenant. Specific asks from our side:

- Minimum outbound ports/IPs required (hec/syslog/s3/kafka endpoints) and whether connections are client-initiated or need persistent tunnels.
- If mutual TLS is required for HEC; cert rotation expectations and supported CA options.
- A short-runbook for incident evidence transfer (how we request packetized evidence, transport, retention).
- Any on-host agents vs. forwarder preference for private on-prem installs.

We attached our internal compliance checklist. Can you confirm what you need from our infra team to run the onramp? Also please include sample firewall rules and a sample allowlist.

Pat
Ops Lead, Acme Logistics

Attachment: acme-compliance-checklist.docx (docx)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 17:05:44 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — thanks for the clear list. I pulled together a concise network + observability handoff that should map to the checklist. High level answers first, then a proposed runbook and attachments.

High level:
- Outbound connections are client-initiated from the private control plane to our ingest endpoints. No inbound firewall holes are required into customer networks.
- Supported transports: HTTPS HEC (Splunk), TLS-wrapped syslog over TCP, S3-compatible object push for batch evidence, and Kafka (TLS) where customers already host a broker.
- HEC: mutual TLS is supported and recommended for higher assurance; we also support token-based HEC over TLS if MTLS is operationally difficult. Cert rotation: we recommend 90-day rotation with automated renewal via your internal CA or HashiVault — we document the API hooks in the attached checklist.
- Forwarder pattern: preferred model is a lightweight forwarder (constrained container) that performs local buffering, checksum bundling and signs evidence packets before pushing to Redwood-controlled ingest endpoints. This minimizes required changes to customer SIEM.

Proposed firewall allowlist (egress only) — minimal set (sample):
- 55.116.210.10/32 TCP 443 (redwood-hec-prod-1.redwood.com) — Splunk HEC endpoint for tenant ingest
- 52.18.34.0/24 TCP 443 (redwood-metrics-prod.redwood.com) — metrics push endpoint (Prometheus remote write over TLS)
- 34.201.12.87/32 TCP 514 (syslog-tls.redwood.com) — TCP/TLS syslog aggregator (optional)
- 13.59.23.128/32 TCP 443 (s3-redwood-evidence.us-west-2.amazonaws.com) — S3-compatible evidence bucket interface
- NTP outbound UDP 123 to your internal NTP or allowed pool (time drift impacts signed evidence)

Notes: we provide static egress IPs for Dedicated/private contracts; if you're using ephemeral NAT IPs we recommend setting up a small NAT pool and letting us know the range so we can update ACLs.

Runbook (short):
1) Pre-onramp: exchange tenant IDs, public keys for MTLS, and preferred ingest mode (HEC vs S3).
2) Networking: customer provisions egress ACLs for the allowlist (sample attached).
3) Deployment: we supply a forwarder container image and Helm chart (on-prem) or systemd unit (bare metal).
4) Acceptance: push a signed evidence packet and verify checksums; complete basic trace correlation sample (span id preserved).
5) Production: enable continuous metrics remote-write, set retention windows, and onboard into your Splunk index with provided field mappings.

Attachments: redwood-network-nexus-diagram-v2.pdf — includes sample topology with peering/VPN variants; siem-handshake-checklist.xlsx — mapping of checklist items to delivery owners; egress-allowlist.txt — copy-pasteable ACLs.

I'll loop in Jordan for the trace hook specifics and Marissa for the acceptance criteria. If you want, we can schedule a 30-min run-through with your infra team tomorrow afternoon.

— Aditya
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 09:48:22 -0400

- For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)? We will not share private keys; we can publish an intermediate CA bundle.
- Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json that includes UTC timestamps, sha256 checksums for each log/trace file, and the forwarder signature. Do you provide tooling to validate signatures client-side?
- Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs and preservation of trace/span metadata when exported to Splunk; our APM team will validate with a small trace run.

Also, Splunk onboarding asked whether you prefer to map logs into a neutral JSON schema prior to HEC ingest or to rely on field extraction rules inside Splunk. Their concern is consistent parsing across environments.

Can we also get the brief runbook inserted into our internal ticket system (I've attached the ticket) so our network team can pick it up.

title: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Diana —

Thanks again for the time last week. As we talked through during the demo, the fastest path for Medicore to get a short pilot live (1–2 weeks of ingest + 2 weeks evaluation) is if we align on a light MAP that sequences technical acceptance, security evidence, and signature gates.

I pulled together a first-pass MAP (attached) that maps out the:
- Evaluation milestones (data ingest, baseline eval, accuracy check, UAT)
- Contract gating (MSA signature, DPA completion, order form sign-off)
- Procurement items (SOW/PO, billing contact, payment terms)

Can you confirm these constraints from your side:
1) Legal needs a full DPA review by Medicore legal before MSA countersign — true/false?
2) Procurement requires an internal PO number before vendor activation?
3) Any absolute blackout dates for June/July that would block your security team?

If that lines up, Karthik (cc) and I will propose a minimal sequencing that keeps the evaluation independent of final order-form language until you hit the UAT pass. Attached: medicore_map_v1.xlsx (MAP draft).

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0126
Redwood Inference

Attachments: medicore_map_v1.xlsx (MAP draft)

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@medicore.com>, Elaine Wu <elaine.wu@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the MAP. Quick answers from procurement/legal side:
- DPA: Legal prefers to agree DPA terms before any provisioning for data containing PHI. For a limited pilot we can consider a scoped data subset and a short-form DPA addendum, but Legal (Sanjay) needs to review first.
- PO: Procurement typically issues PO before we process payment, but IT sometimes allows a temporary vendor onboarding form that lets us stand up test accounts for pilots. I'll check if we can do a temporary onboarding to start ingest without full PO.
- Blackouts: Our security reviewers are away Jul 3–10 (conference travel). Any gating work in that window will slow us down.

Sanjay, Elaine — looping you in to confirm DPA path.

Best,
Diana Park
Director, Product Operations | Medicore Health
D: 212-555-0444

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 08:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Diana / Ava —

Thanks for the clarity. Based on your constraints I recommend a two-track sequencing that minimizes legal blocking for the pilot provisioning:

Track A (Technical onboarding — low legal friction) — timeline: Day 0–10
- Day 0: Temporary vendor onboarding form signed by Medicore IT (no PO) -> Redwood provisions sandbox tenant (no PHI)
- Day 1–4: Data subset ingest (de-identified) + baseline eval run
- Day 5–8: Joint review of metrics; iterate prompts/configs
- Day 9–10: UAT pass for feature set we agreed as pilot success

Track B (Contracting & procurement) — runs in parallel, aim to finish by Day 14
- Day 0–7: Medicore legal reviews short-form DPA addendum + Redwood sends MSA summary (redwood_msa_summary.pdf attached)
- Day 8–12: Procurement issues PO / order form (or provisional PO)
- Day 12–14: Signatures complete, move pilot to production tenant if desired

Key assumptions / asks from Medicore:
- If Legal insists on full DPA prior to any provisioning we will adjust and keep initial runs strictly on synthetic/de-identified data.
- Provide a single procurement contact and billing email to speed PO issuance.

I'll attach a checklist CSV for our side (procurement_checklist.csv) to track items. Marcus — can you review the acceptance gating bullets (below) and confirm they look OK to ship as the MAP success criteria?

Acceptance gating (proposal):
- Data ingest complete (ingested rows match sample schema)
- Top-line latency and throughput within target (95thp < 350ms)
- Baseline quality: precision/recall thresholds met on test set
- No P0 security flags during vetting

Thanks,
Karthik
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_msa_summary.pdf, procurement_checklist.csv

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jun 2025 14:06:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

All —

I reviewed the acceptance gating and have two small edits to keep the pilot focused and unblock signature sequencing:
1) Split the "quality" gate into two: baseline metric pass for the instrumented test set (used for internal validation) and a lightweight business-signoff milestone (Medicore product owner confirms results are "fit for purpose"). This lets Legal/Procurement sign the order-form while business-side confirms fit.
2) Add a contingency: if Medicore legal requests changes to the DPA that materially affect data processing, we will cap the pilot to de-identified data and pause PHI-related evaluations until DPA is agreed.

If you (Diana/Sanjay) can confirm the temporary onboarding form option, I propose we target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 and run the technical Track A in parallel with contracting Track B through Jul 10 (noting your blackout Jul 3–10). That timing keeps the evaluation window clear of major holidays and aligns with our engineering squad availability.

Marcus Lin
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0899

title: Benchmark report launch — lifecycle email A/B plan (segments + success metrics)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Jun 30, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Benchmark report launch — lifecycle email A/B plan (segments + success metrics)

Team — sharing a proposed A/B plan for the benchmark report launch lifecycle emails. Goal is to drive (1) self-serve signups + activation and (2) qualified demo requests for Dedicated/Private.

Asks for today:
1) Tyler: can you sanity-check segmentation feasibility + list sizes + timing constraints (deliverability / throttling)?
2) Isabelle: confirm attribution + success metric definitions + how we’ll read “influenced pipeline” in HubSpot.
3) Soojin: confirm copy is aligned with claims/disclaimers language (esp. “latency/$”).
4) Grace: confirm CTA + SDR routing for the enterprise segment.

Current proposal (high level)
- Segment A (PLG active): last 30d API usage > 0, no sales owner → CTA: “View interactive benchmark” + “Try model in 5 minutes”.
- Segment B (PLG inactive): signed up, no usage last 30d → CTA: “Get 10k free tokens + see the fastest regions”.
- Segment C (enterprise leads): known company email, job function Eng/ML/Platform, or existing HubSpot Lead Status ≠ Disqualified → CTA: “Get a Dedicated sizing consult” + “Talk to Solutions”.

A/B tests (subject + hero framing)
- A: Subject leans technical/transparent. B: Subject leans outcome/cost.
- Variant 1 hero: “Latency per dollar (p50/p95 + $/1M tokens)”
- Variant 2 hero: “Production inference economics across regions”

Success metrics (draft)
- Primary: click-to-activation (signup → first API request within 7d) for Seg A/B; demo request submission rate for Seg C.
- Secondary: CTR, benchmark page engagement, export clicks, and “Contact sales” click.

Attachments:
- launch-email-copy-v1.docx (draft copy w/ placeholders)
- benchmark_launch_email_ab_test_plan_v1.xlsx (segments, splits, metrics)

Ben
—
Ben Carter
Growth Campaign Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Jun 30, 2025 at 10:01 AM PT
Subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — lifecycle email A/B plan (segments + success metrics)

Feasible, with a couple constraints:

Segmentation/list sizes (rough, as of this morning):
- Seg A (PLG active 30d): ~18.4k contacts (email-able). 
- Seg B (PLG inactive 30d): ~41.2k contacts (email-able).
- Seg C (enterprise leads): ~6.1k contacts (email-able).

Notes:
