title: Order form questions: auto-renew, notice windows, and termination for convenience
preview:
From: Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for sending the draft order form. A couple of legal/procurement items came up on our side that will affect procurement signoff — can you confirm Redwood's position on the following?

1) Auto-renew term: The draft shows a 12-month auto-renew. Our procurement team prefers a rolling 6-month renewal (or a 12-month with a 60-day opt-out). Is Redwood able to accommodate either of these?

2) Notice window for non-renewal: We typically require a 60-day notice to avoid auto-renew. The order form language currently says 30 days. Can Redwood match 60 days?

3) Termination for convenience: Our legal team has asked for a defined wind-down period and an indication of whether any early-exit fees apply if we exercise termination for convenience mid-term.

4) Pilot carve-out: We are running a small pilot that we would like to grandfather — i.e., if we opt to terminate the main order, we'd like the pilot workload to continue for 90 days to allow migration.

I've attached the order form we edited with comments (Acme_OrderForm_Draft.pdf). Can we get Redwood's proposed position/alternative redlines by EOD Thursday? If helpful, happy to hop on a quick call.

Thanks,
Samiha
Samiha Khan
Head of Procurement — Acme Apps
Attachment: Acme_OrderForm_Draft.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Hi Samiha — thanks for the clear notes and the annotated order form.

I'll loop in Legal and Sales Ops to give concrete redline options. High level: Redwood is open to discussing a 60-day non-renew notice; the termination-for-convenience treatment has some internal guardrails (we usually allow TFc but expect a short wind-down and pro-rated billing). I want Legal to propose exact language and any constraints (e.g., minimum notice periods, impact on reserved capacity).

Aditya/Laura/Ben — FYI and please propose one conservative redline and one compromise position we can share with Acme. Samiha — can you confirm whether the pilot site is on a separate order or covered by the same order form? That will determine whether we can grandfather it without formal amendment.

Propose a 30-minute call Wednesday 10am PT if that's possible.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions: renewal & termination (redline proposals)

Hi all — reviewed Acme's annotated order form and sketched redlines. Attaching: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf (high level notes below).

Summary of proposed positions (two tracks):

1) Conservative (Legal-preferred)
- Auto-renew: Keep 12-month auto-renew but add mutual written notice requirement 60 days before renewal effective date; customer may opt-out only if all outstanding invoices are settled.
- Notice window: 60 days for non-renewal is acceptable if paired with a minimum early termination notice to allow re-provisioning of dedicated or reserved capacity.
- Termination for convenience (TFC): Allow mutual TFC with 90 days' written notice and pro-rated service credit for unused committed term. Exclude pilot accounts from the TFC carve-out unless explicitly added to an amendment.
- Pilot carve-out: We can agree to grandfather pilot workloads for up to 90 days provided they are low-utilization and do not require dedicated reserved capacity.

2) Compromise (Commercial)
- Auto-renew: Change auto-renew to 6 months for orders under $150k ARR; otherwise 12-month auto-renew with 60-day opt-out.
- Notice window: Accept 60 days across the board.
- TFC: Allow Customer TFC with 60 days' notice plus a termination fee equal to 1 month of average monthly spend for the prior 3 months if terminated mid-term. Redwood to provide transition assistance for 30 days. Pilot workloads would be grandfathered for 90 days on a case-by-case basis.

Practical constraints & ask from Acme:
- If the customer's order uses Redwood Dedicated/reserved GPU capacity, any mid-term TFC is disruptive; we would need at least 90 days to reassign capacity or provide a pro-rata settlement with minimum payment for resource reservation.
- Please confirm whether Acme's pilot is on the same ODF/order line and approximate monthly token usage for the pilot (estimate fine).

Next steps: If Acme prefers the Commercial compromise we'll prepare an amendment to the order form. If Legal prefers the Conservative track we'll push that as the proposed redline. Either way I'll prepare language by EOD Wednesday and propose the 30-minute sync.

Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf (summary redline with struck/added language)

Regards,
Aditya Rao
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf


From: Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <t.nguyen@acmeapps.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 15:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Thanks, Aditya — that is helpful. Quick answers:

- The pilot is on the same order and currently running on standard hosted capacity (not Dedicated). Monthly estimated spend for the pilot is ~3k USD and token usage is low.
- Procurement will accept the compromise track if Redwood can commit to grandfathering the pilot for 90 days and explicitly waive any termination fee for the pilot during that period.
- For the main order, Acme prefers the 6-month auto-renew for orders <$150k and 60-day non-renew notice. The 1-month termination fee for mid-term TFc is likely acceptable provided we get 30 days of migration assistance.

If you can circulate the commercial compromise redline by Wednesday EOD we can share with our GC and procurement and aim for signature the following week.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — Tom and I are available for the Wednesday 10am PT call.

Best,
Samiha
Head of Procurement — Acme Apps

- Auto-renew: Keep 12-month auto-renew but add mutual written notice requirement 60 days before renewal effective date; customer may opt-out only if all outstanding invoices are settled.
- Notice window: 60 days for non-renewal is acceptable if paired with a minimum early termination notice to allow re-provisioning of dedicated or reserved capacity.
- Termination for convenience (TFC): Allow mutual TFC with 90 days' written notice and pro-rated service credit for unused committed term. Exclude pilot accounts from the TFC carve-out unless explicitly added to an amendment.
- Pilot carve-out: We can agree to grandfather pilot workloads for up to 90 days provided they are low-utilization and do not require dedicated reserved capacity.

2) Compromise (Commercial)
- Auto-renew: Change auto-renew to 6 months for orders under $150k ARR; otherwise 12-month auto-renew with 60-day opt-out.
- Notice window: Accept 60 days across the board.
- TFC: Allow Customer TFC with 60 days' notice plus a termination fee equal to 1 month of average monthly spend for the prior 3 months if terminated mid-term. Redwood to provide transition assistance for 30 days. Pilot workloads would be grandfathered for 90 days on a case-by-case basis.

Practical constraints & ask from Acme:
- If the customer's order uses Redwood Dedicated/reserved GPU capacity, any mid-term TFC is disruptive; we would need at least 90 days to reassign capacity or provide a pro-rata settlement with minimum payment for resource reservation.
- Please confirm whether Acme's pilot is on the same ODF/order line and approximate monthly token usage for the pilot (estimate fine).

Next steps: If Acme prefers the Commercial compromise we'll prepare an amendment to the order form. If Legal prefers the Conservative track we'll push that as the proposed redline. Either way I'll prepare language by EOD Wednesday and propose the 30-minute sync.

Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf (summary redline with struck/added language)

Regards,
Aditya Rao
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf

title: MAP: PromptBank handoff — sandbox gating and audit cadence
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alice Moore <alice@acmeretail.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Jun 2025 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: PromptBank handoff — sandbox gating and audit cadence

Hi Alice —

Following our demo, attached is a draft mutual action plan (MAP) and the first prompt pack sample we discussed. High level: we want to get you an ephemeral sandbox with a locked-down PromptBank (~2k testcases + metadata) so your engineering team can run acceptance tests without persistent custody. Key constraints and asks below so we can finalize the schedule: 

Proposed MAP milestones (draft) — please confirm: 
- Week 0 (today): sign MAP + data-handling addendum (PDF attached).
- Day 2: Redacted prompt pack (promptbank_v1.zip, prompts scrubbed for direct PII) delivered to sandbox.
- Day 3-10: Acceptance run by Acme (sample_testcases_2000.jsonl) against our runtime; lightweight quality checks.
- Day 11: Feedback window — list of failing cases and pass threshold sign-off.
- Day 14: If accepted, provide short-term access tokens for extended pilot (ephemeral, auto-rotated).

Usage constraints (non-negotiable items we include in the MAP):
- No redistribution of raw prompt/test assets outside Acme engineering team.
- No training or fine-tuning on the provided promptpack (explicitly prohibited).
- Audit logging enabled for all sandbox requests (retention: 30 days by default; can extend by agreement).
- Sandbox is rate-limited; we'll share a traffic profile to match your acceptance run.

Attachments: MAP_Acme_Redwood_20250619.pdf (MAP + handoff checklist), promptbank_v1.zip (sample, redacted), sample_testcases_2000.jsonl (testcases).

If this looks good I can: (a) open a HubSpot deal milestone with the schedule, (b) kick off legal with the MAP, and (c) arrange a short onboarding call with our platform engineer to provision the sandbox. Suggested timeline: acceptance run completed by June 30.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0123

Ben —

Appreciate the clear plan. A few quick clarifying questions from our side before we route to Legal: 

1) PII: you mentioned "redacted" prompts — can you confirm the scrub process and whether any tokens of customer PII might be present in the testcases? If so, can those be hashed/pseudonymized instead of included in cleartext?
2) Retention: 30 days is fine as a starting point, but can we get an option to extend to 90 days for audit purposes? We'll need to check with compliance.
3) Training: to be explicit — Acme's security team will want the clause that prohibits training/fine-tuning on the provided assets. That must be non-negotiable for our procurement.
4) Access: can you provide SSO or at minimum SAML-based access for our engineers to the sandbox?

We can aim to run the acceptance tests the week of June 23 — June 30 target sounds okay provided the legal addendum is quick. I've cc'd prod-legal at Acme to loop them in.

Thanks,
Alice

Director, Product Analytics — Acme Retail
alice@acmeretail.com

Date: Fri, 20 Jun 2025 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: PromptBank handoff — sandbox gating and audit cadence

Thanks Alice — quick answers inline and next steps.

PII/scrub: our redaction pipeline tokenizes and removes obvious PII categories (names, emails, phone numbers). We replace entities with deterministic pseudonyms (e.g., cust_0001) so your QA can still reason about persona-level behavior without raw PII. I'll attach a one-page scrub description to the MAP PDF.

Retention: yes — we can offer 30 days by default and a configurable extension to 90 days for customers on the pilot. We flag extended retention as an opt-in in the MAP.

Training clause: understood — the MAP includes an explicit "no training / no fine-tuning" clause. We'll highlight that for your legal.

Access: for the pilot we provide SAML SSO for your engineers; platform engineer will onboard two test accounts during provisioning.

Proposed next steps (please confirm):
- You: route MAP_Acme_Redwood_20250619.pdf to procurement/legal for redlines.
- We: provision the redacted prompt pack into a read-only sandbox (ephemeral keys) within 48 hours of signed MAP.
- Joint: 1-hour onboarding call (Platform eng + your eng lead) to validate test harness and traffic profile.

HubSpot: I logged the deal and milestones here: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/ACME-2025-01 — I will update as milestones complete.

Copying Laura on legal next steps. If you want, I can also send a DocuSign for the MAP so legal can redline in parallel.

Best,
Ben

Subject: Re: MAP: PromptBank handoff — sandbox gating and audit cadence

Ben —

Appreciate the clear plan. A few quick clarifying questions from our side before we route to Legal: 

1) PII: you mentioned "redacted" prompts — can you confirm the scrub process and whether any tokens of customer PII might be present in the testcases? If so, can those be hashed/pseudonymized instead of included in cleartext?
2) Retention: 30 days is fine as a starting point, but can we get an option to extend to 90 days for audit purposes? We'll need to check with compliance.
3) Training: to be explicit — Acme's security team will want the clause that prohibits training/fine-tuning on the provided assets. That must be non-negotiable for our procurement.
4) Access: can you provide SSO or at minimum SAML-based access for our engineers to the sandbox?

We can aim to run the acceptance tests the week of June 23 — June 30 target sounds okay provided the legal addendum is quick. I've cc'd prod-legal at Acme to loop them in.

Thanks,
Alice

Director, Product Analytics — Acme Retail
alice@acmeretail.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Fri, 20 Jun 2025 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: PromptBank handoff — sandbox gating and audit cadence

Thanks Alice — quick answers inline and next steps.

PII/scrub: our redaction pipeline tokenizes and removes obvious PII categories (names, emails, phone numbers). We replace entities with deterministic pseudonyms (e.g., cust_0001) so your QA can still reason about persona-level behavior without raw PII. I'll attach a one-page scrub description to the MAP PDF.

Retention: yes — we can offer 30 days by default and a configurable extension to 90 days for customers on the pilot. We flag extended retention as an opt-in in the MAP.

Training clause: understood — the MAP includes an explicit "no training / no fine-tuning" clause. We'll highlight that for your legal.

Access: for the pilot we provide SAML SSO for your engineers; platform engineer will onboard two test accounts during provisioning.

Proposed next steps (please confirm):
- You: route MAP_Acme_Redwood_20250619.pdf to procurement/legal for redlines.
- We: provision the redacted prompt pack into a read-only sandbox (ephemeral keys) within 48 hours of signed MAP.
- Joint: 1-hour onboarding call (Platform eng + your eng lead) to validate test harness and traffic profile.

HubSpot: I logged the deal and milestones here: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/ACME-2025-01 — I will update as milestones complete.

Copying Laura on legal next steps. If you want, I can also send a DocuSign for the MAP so legal can redline in parallel.

Best,
Ben

- Recommend increasing default audit log retention to 60 days for pilots where customers ask (we can offer 30/60/90 tiers).

title: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 21 Jun 2025 14:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Hi Alex —

Great to meet earlier and thanks for walking us through BrightCart's ingestion flow and expected throughput. Quick recap + proposed next steps from our side: 

- Fit: Hosted API looks like the fastest onramp for your use case (no infra lift), with Dedicated as an option once you hit sustained peak traffic or need guaranteed capacity.
- Key asks we flagged: expected peak concurrent requests (~40 qps), burst patterns (short spikes up to ~150 qps during promotions), and data residency for EU shoppers (you mentioned EU region requirement for parts of the service).

Next steps we propose: 
1) We’ll provision a sandbox Hosted endpoint for you — endpoint details and token below. This will be rate-limited by default; we’ll propose a rate plan and a temporary higher burst slice for the pilot.
2) Share a one-page quickstart checklist and a small sample endpoint capacity CSV for you to validate against your traffic.
3) If you want predictable latency/SLA later, we can sketch a Dedicated capacity baseline and estimated monthly commit.

I’ll loop Ben for the concrete ratebands and Marissa for the onboarding runbook. Expect the sandbox token + checklists within 24 hours.

Thanks again — looking forward to a short pilot.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/jordan-blake



From: Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Jun 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Jordan —

Thanks for the clear recap. Confirming a few things from our side: 

- Peak and burst numbers are correct (40 qps steady, promo spikes up to 150 qps over a 10–15 minute window).
- We need EU residency for any PII-derived embeddings, but non-PII inference can run in us-west for the pilot.
- Timeline: team can start integration next Tuesday (June 24).

Please send the sandbox token and the quickstart checklist. Also can you include a suggested rateband (tokens/sec and allowlist for burst) so we can validate locally? If Hosted proves noisy in terms of unit cost we may want to test a small Dedicated baseline — happy to get a ballpark.

Cheers,
Alex Turner
Head of Platform Engineering, BrightCart
alex@brightcart.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Jun 2025 08:02:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Looping in suggested numbers for the pilot and an internal checklist: 

Suggested Hosted pilot ratebands (initial):
- Default: 30 qps sustained, per-endpoint token throttle at 15 tokens/sec (keeps latency predictable)
- Pilot burst slice: 120 qps for 30 minutes/day window for two weeks
- Backpressure: 429 + Retry-After header for sustained exceed; circuit-breaker at 200 qps sustained per endpoint

Notes: these are conservative so BrightCart can validate behavior without overrun. If they confirm traffic profile we can increase sustained limits or propose Dedicated estimates (rough calc below).

Dedicated baseline (rough, for planning only):
- GPU pool sized for 200 qps steady (mixed short-context gen) ≈ 2 x A10-equivalent nodes reserved; estimated commit ~$18k/month (subject to model/quantization).

Attachments I’m dropping here: redwood-quickstart-checklist.pdf and sample-endpoint-capacity-plan.csv — includes a sample mapping of endpoints to expected qps and a checklist for keys, CORS, and prefetched KV cache entries.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai



From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Jun 2025 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Alex —

Thanks — confirmed on the timeline. Attached are the two files Ben mentioned. Quick pointers and the sandbox token below: 

- Sandbox endpoint: https://api.redwood.ai/v1/hosted/brightcart-sandbox (token: sk_test_brightcart_20250623)
- Rate plan: we provisioned the initial default and the pilot burst slice Ben suggested. You’ll see 429 responses with Retry-After when the burst window is exhausted.
- EU residency: we’ve enabled geo-routing for embeddings so PII-derived embeddings can be routed to eu-central-1. Engineers will need to set region=eu in the request header for those calls.

Requested next actions: 
1) BrightCart: validate the attached sample-endpoint-capacity-plan.csv against your traffic (highlight any endpoints >120 qps).
2) BrightCart: attempt a small integration on June 24 and share any 429 patterns + example requests.
3) Redwood: we’ll prepare a short Dedicated sketch if you ask for cost-comparison after the pilot.

I’ll also drop the HubSpot link for the deal and the DocuSign for a mutual NDA if you prefer it signed before sending sample data.

HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.ai/deal/BRIGHT-1294
DocuSign NDA (optional): https://www.docusign.com/envelopes/E-123456789

Look forward to Tuesday — ping me on Slack if you want the sandbox rotated or a wider burst window.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0134
Redwood Inference — Production-first inference for builders

title: Fleet orientation + weekly pipeline-to-GPU sync
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Fleet orientation + weekly pipeline-to-GPU sync

Hi team,

For next week I'd like to start a short weekly sync (30 min) to align Sales/Finance/Infra on the pipeline -> dedicated capacity sizing. Main goals:
- Share updated close timing and committed vs. best-case pipeline per deal
- Translate deal forecasts into GPU slot demand by month (Q3 calendar)
- Surface infra constraints (preferred GPU families, burst headroom) and cost expectations from FinOps

Can you each upload the latest pipeline worksheet and a 1-slide note on hard constraints to the Drive folder below by EOD Friday? I attached the orientation deck we iterate from and a working forecast template.

Proposed standing time: Tuesdays 8:30am PT. First session: Tue 24 Jun.

Attachments: pipeline_forecast_Q3.xlsx (template), orientation_deck_v2.pdf (1-slide orientation)

Thanks — Avery
Avery Johnson
Head of Sales Ops (acting)
Redwood Inference

Thanks Avery — I'm in. I've attached a CSV export of my deal-level forecast (pipeline_by-deal.csv) and a short table in the body with the near-term asks. Highlights:

- Q3 expected demand (consolidated): 420K token-hours equivalent (~75 H100-slot-months if fully reserved)
- Top-3 priority closes (Jun–Aug): Acme pilot (50% conv window in July), Nimbus expansion (commit by Aug), Orion pilot (late Aug)
- Committed baseline (already contract-signed): 20 H100-slot-months across existing pilots

Open question for infra: how much burst headroom can we promise for pilots vs reserved capacity? Also tagging in FinOps question: do we need a pre-approval step for >$50K incremental monthly spend?

Attachment: pipeline_by-deal.csv

Laura Bennett
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Jun 2025 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fleet orientation + weekly pipeline-to-GPU sync

Quick infra note — thanks for kicking this off.

- Preferred families: H100 for latency-sensitive inference; A100 (or older Ampere quantized) for large-batch re-ranking/embeddings where latency can be relaxed.
- Current burst pool: ~10 H100-equivalent slots available for non-reserved bursts across the US region (next refill in ~11 days).
- Lead time: provisioning reserved pools takes ~3–4 weeks (procurement + capacity carve). If Acme pilot is likely to convert in July, we should decide by 1 Jul to hit July reserved start.

If Sales can indicate the per-deal committed slot-months (best guess), Infra can slot a staged reserve + burst plan. I'm attaching a short note on allocation rules (attachment stub — infra_allocation_rules.md).

Ben Carter
Principal Ops PM, Fleet Planning


From: Sarah Lin <sarah.lin@acme.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Jun 2025 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fleet orientation + weekly pipeline-to-GPU sync

Hi all — Sarah from Acme FinOps. Thanks for the heads up. Two quick asks from our side before commit:
1) Estimated monthly cost delta for the 50 H100-slot-months example Laura noted (breakdown by reserved vs burst).
2) SLA / fallback policy if reserved capacity experiences preemption or region outages (we need to present this to procurement).

We prefer a short call with Priyom to nail the cost model — available Mon 23 Jun afternoon PT.

Sarah Lin
Head of FinOps, Acme Corp

- Ben: draft burst/reserved staging plan and allocation rules doc by Tue 24 Jun
- Priyom: schedule 30-min cost deep-dive with Acme (Sarah) for Mon 23 Jun 2:30pm PT (Priyom, please confirm)

Cheers — Avery

title: Proposed MAP, success definitions & timeline to commit
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests

Hi Sara —

Great to meet last week. Per our conversation I attached a first-pass mutual action plan (MAP) and an \"acceptance tests\" draft that maps the success criteria to concrete checks we can run during the 4-week evaluation. Key points up front:

- Goal: Validate integration with existing ClearPath EHR pipeline + achieve >92% classification concordance on our canonical prompt set, and maintain P95 latency < 320ms for 512-token responses.
- Exit gate (go): evidence of functional integration, two weeks of steady concordance >= 92% on the evaluation set, and no SEV-level incidents during the trial window.
- Exit gate (no-go): concordance < 88% for the majority of the rollout tests OR average token cost > projected threshold after optimization (we'll show cost projections).

Attachments: ClearPath_MAP_v1.xlsx, acceptance_tests_v1.md (both in this thread).

Can you review the attached MAP and acceptance tests and let us know where procurement and security want evidence (SOC2/pen test artifacts)? If you're OK with the gates above we can lock the timeline and get Legal/Procurement involved for a PO/SOW.

Proposed next steps:
1) You review and flag any missing acceptance checks (by Tue 6/17).
2) We deliver a lightweight smoke-test pack (we'll attach) and a runbook for test execution.
3) Kickoff pilot week of 6/23, 4-week evaluation window, decision call w/ execs in week 4.

Thanks — happy to jump on a 30-minute call if that helps.

— Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference



From: Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Daniel Lowe <daniel.lowe@clearpathhealth.com>, ClearPath Health Procurement <procurement@clearpathhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Jun 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests

Ben, thanks — I skimmed both attachments and will circulate to Daniel (clinical lead) and procurement. A couple quick asks:

- Can you add an explicit data isolation check (we need to confirm data never persists outside our VPC during the pilot)?
- We need SOC 2 Type II evidence and recent pen test summary to share with security — can you point me to those artifacts?
- Procurement requests an SOW/template pricing that aligns to the MAP milestones so we can start internal approvals.

We like the proposed timeline but need to align procurement calendar: our purchasing blackout is 6/25–6/29 (month-end approvals), so if the pilot kickoff is 6/23 we may not have a PO until early July. Can the pilot proceed on an MSA + temporary credit while procurement finalizes the PO?

I'll review acceptance test specifics with Daniel and come back with comments by Monday.

— Sara
Director, Clinical Innovation
ClearPath Health



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jun 2025 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests

Sara — appreciate the quick review and the procurement heads-up. A few clarifications inline and below.

On data isolation: Redwood Private can be configured for VPC-only egress for the pilot. I'll attach a short evidence pack and a link to our SOC2 summary (see drive link in related links) — we can provide an MSA + refundable credits for the pilot while PO is in flight; legal has approved this pattern 90% of the time.

Quick, concrete acceptance checklist (short version):
- Integration: API end-to-end ingest -> model response -> destination mapped; three sample patient journeys verified. (Owner: ClearPath + Redwood infra)
- Quality: Concordance > 92% on pre-shared canonical prompt set (run nightly across pilot window).
- Reliability: P95 inference latency <= 320ms for 512-token baseline under pilot load profile (we'll provide the load harness).
- Safety/Failover: fallthrough behavior verified when model unavailable (auto-fallback to baseline model, no adverse clinical outputs).
- Observability: token-level cost visibility and per-route latency traces enabled at test start.

If you and Daniel are comfortable with those as the primary gates we will:
1) Finalize MAP spreadsheet and acceptance_tests_v1.md (attached).
2) Deliver smoke-tests.yaml and latency-signals.csv to run the initial tech validation (attachments incoming from Marissa).
3) Offer the MSA + pilot-credit option to cover the short procurement gap.

Proposed decision cadence to commit:
- Week 0 (kickoff): baseline integration + test harness run.
- Weeks 1–3: metric collection and triage.
- Week 4: decision call (Go/No-go). If Go, begin SOW/PO handoff and production onboarding cadence.

I'll also add a short rollback clause we recommend (if concordance drops to <88% after any major config change, we'll pause the rollout and revert to previous model).

On SOC2/pen test evidence: see the shared link and we'll ship a redacted pen-test summary and our SOC2 attestation this afternoon.

— Ben

On Fri, Jun 13, 2025 at 11:05 AM Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com> wrote:
> Ben, thanks — I skimmed both attachments and will circulate to Daniel (clinical lead) and procurement.
> ...



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sara Perez <sara.perez@clearpathhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP, initial success criteria & acceptance tests — smoke test details

Hi all — piggybacking on Ben's checklist with the engineering/QA acceptance tests we'll run during the pilot. Attaching smoke-tests.yaml and latency-signals.csv. Highlights and execution notes:

- Smoke test suite (execution window: kickoff day):
  * Auth/ingest: 10 synthetic payloads through the real integration pipeline; confirm no PII persists in logs.
  * End-to-end functional: 5 real-case samples (redacted) with expected structured output schema.
  * Failover: simulate model node loss and confirm fallback path within 2s and no malformed clinical output.

- Metric thresholds to mark test pass/fail during pilot:
  * Concordance: pass >= 92%, caution 88–92%, fail < 88%.
  * Latency: pass P95 <= 320ms, caution 320–380ms, fail > 380ms.
  * Cost per 1k tokens: we will calculate against your expected query volume; we'll flag if >10% above projection.

- Rollback conditions: any single-day concordance drop below 85% triggers automatic pause and rollback; repeated SEV incidents (>=2 within 7 days) triggers pilot suspension.

Operational notes: we'll need a ClearPath point-of-contact to run the smoke-suite and provide logs for the first two days (we recommend Daniel or a SW engineer). Also please confirm which environment (staging vs pre-prod) you'll prefer for the pilot.

Attachments: smoke-tests.yaml (runbook), latency-signals.csv (load profile).

— Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineering, Redwood Inference

1) A statement of work (SOW) or template SOW that matches the MAP milestones and includes payment milestones tied to the acceptance gates.
2) A redacted SOC2 Type II report or letter of attestation + proof of current cyber insurance.
3) Pricing spreadsheet (preferably milestone-based) and your preferred PO terms (Net30/Net45).

If you can provide drafts of the SOW and pricing by 6/20 we can route for an expedited review. Otherwise the earliest we can approve a PO is 7/2 due to our internal blackout week. For the pilot, is Redwood able to proceed on a temporary credit arrangement (as Ben suggested) pending PO?

Thanks — procurement team
ClearPath Health

  * Cost per 1k tokens: we will calculate against your expected query volume; we'll flag if >10% above projection.

- Rollback conditions: any single-day concordance drop below 85% triggers automatic pause and rollback; repeated SEV incidents (>=2 within 7 days) triggers pilot suspension.

Operational notes: we'll need a ClearPath point-of-contact to run the smoke-suite and provide logs for the first two days (we recommend Daniel or a SW engineer). Also please confirm which environment (staging vs pre-prod) you'll prefer for the pilot.

Attachments: smoke-tests.yaml (runbook), latency-signals.csv (load profile).

— Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineering, Redwood Inference

title: Deal-GPU matchup & weekly scouting

Subject: Deal-GPU matchup & weekly scouting

Morning team —

Following up from Friday's pipeline review: Acme is moving into pilot planning and finance wants a tighter view on GPU runway tied to their expected usage profile. I attached the latest pipeline forecast from Sales Ops (acme_pipeline_forecast.xlsx).

Asks: - Ben / Infra: can you sketch a conservative and an aggressive slot allocation that would cover the pilot + 25% buffer for testing? Include burst eligibility assumptions.
- Laura / Finance: can you surface the expected committed spend window we should tie to a reserved slot ask? (Q3 vs Q4 timing)
- Marissa / Sales Ops: help confirm start date assumptions and whether Acme will require dedicated pool isolation for data controls.

Timing: I'd like a short triage on Monday that produces a one-page matchup we can show to Acme (they asked for capacity lock by end of week).

Attachments: acme_pipeline_forecast.xlsx

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Sr. Strategic Partnerships, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com

>

Thanks Camila — looked through the forecast quick. Two immediate notes:

1) Based on their projected 5M tok/day peak during pilot, the conservative allocation (target 95th perf) would be:
   - Reserved: 8 x A100-equivalent slots (sustained)
   - Burst: +4 slots for test peaks (autoscale policy)

2) Aggressive option (lower latency SLO / higher concurrency):
   - Reserved: 12 x A100-equivalent slots
   - Burst: +6 slots

I attached the spreadsheet export with simple qps->slot mapping (gpu_request_v1.csv). For either option we need a burn estimate from Finance so we can translate reserved slots into dollars and term length. Also flag: there are only limited free burst slots in the us-west pool in the June window; if Acme needs lock-in we should prioritize their cadence this sprint.

--
Ben Carter
Principal, Infrastructure Partnerships
ben@redwood.com
Attachment: gpu_request_v1.csv


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-GPU matchup & weekly scouting

Camila / Ben —

Quick finance read: if we assume the conservative allocation Ben outlined (8 reserved + 4 burst) and Acme signs a 3-month pilot with a standard discount tier, the expected committed spend is roughly $210k/month (approx). If they want the aggressive posture (12 + 6) that jumps to ~$320k/month.

Two follow-ups I need to finalize numbers:
- Confirm whether burst will be billed on usage (on-demand) or if we need to pre-sell burst credits for price protection.
- Exact pilot start date (affects which billing month counts as first proration).

Attaching a short burn projection matrix I built for Q3 scenarios (burn_projection_q3.pdf). If Marissa can confirm start date assumptions today, I can lock the numbers and we can present both options to Acme with clear cost/latency tradeoffs.

Laura Bennett
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference
laura@redwood.com
Attachment: burn_projection_q3.pdf


From: Priya Sharma <priya@acmeinc.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-GPU matchup & weekly scouting

Thanks team — appreciate the quick turnaround.

Acme confirms target pilot start: July 14, 2025. We'd prefer to lock the conservative allocation now (8 reserved + 4 burst) and we are open to adding burst credits to the contract rather than paying pure on-demand to stabilize cost. Can you send a one-page summary with the two options and the burn numbers by EOD Wednesday? We have our internal procurement review Thursday and would like to see the numbers before then.

Priya Sharma

priya@acmeinc.com

title: BrightArc onboarding: multi-PO invoices, portal ack SLAs, and OCR fallback
preview:
From: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Accounting (BrightArc) <payables@brightarc.io>
Date: Sun, 15 Jun 2025 09:07:12 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks for starting the BrightArc vendor onboarding. A couple of practical questions from AP as we prepare our first invoice: 

1) Our system generates one PDF per invoice but we often need to charge against multiple Redwood POs on the same invoice (separate PO numbers for different teams). Is it acceptable to send a single PDF that contains multiple PO numbers and line items, or do you require one invoice per PO?

2) File naming — would you prefer the PDF subject line and filename to include the primary PO only, or all PO numbers? Example filename we currently use: BrightArc_Invoice_20250614.pdf.

3) Portal vs email: we prefer to submit via SFTP/portal but need a fallback to email while our connector is approved. If we email the invoice, what is your expected acknowledgement window (how quickly will you confirm receipt and expected payment date)?

4) OCR/searchability: our billing engine outputs scanned PDFs that are not searchable. Do you accept a scanned PDF plus a pasted plain-text invoice body in the email as an OCR fallback, or must the PDF itself be text-searchable (PDF/A)?

Attaching a sample invoice so you can see our layout (BrightArc_Inv_20250614.pdf). Happy to adapt — we just want to align before we submit several invoices next week.

Thanks,

Lucy Park
Finance Operations Lead, BrightArc
lucy@brightarc.io
+1 415-555-0182

Attachment: BrightArc_Inv_20250614.pdf (application/pdf, 1.2MB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Jun 2025 11:22:03 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Hi Lucy — great to meet you, and welcome. Short answers first, then some specifics and an attached template that should make multi-PO mapping straightforward.

Summary:
- Portal (preferred): please upload invoices to our Vendor Portal when possible. Portal uploads get an automated acknowledgement within 48 hours and are routed to AP for PO matching.
- Email (fallback): we will accept invoices sent to payables@redwood.ai while you finish connector work, but expect manual processing and a standard acknowledgement timeline of up to 3 business days.
- Multi-PO: single PDF containing multiple PO numbers is acceptable, but you must include a simple CSV or XLSX that maps each invoice line (or subtotal) to the PO number. I attached multi-po-template.csv as an example.
- File naming: include all PO numbers in the email Subject and in the filename where feasible (e.g., BrightArc_INV_20250614_PO1234_PO1250.pdf). If the filename would be too long, include primary PO in the filename and list all POs in the email subject and csv mapping.
- OCR fallback: preferred is a searchable PDF (PDF/A). If you cannot produce that, please paste the line-item table in the email body and attach a CSV/XLSX mapping. That combination will let our OCR/ingest team avoid manual re-keying.

Attached: multi-po-template.csv (CSV showing line->PO mapping) and BrightArc_sample_lines.xlsx (example of how we'd like line items exported).

Couple of operational notes:
- When emailing, put the SUBJECT as: Invoice BrightArc — PO: 1234,1250 — INV: 20250614 -> this helps automated routing.
- For portal uploads, the portal will return an acknowledgement ID and ETA; keep that acknowledgement ID for follow-up.

I'll loop in Marissa (AP) for any special payment-term exceptions; Neha handles vendor setup if you hit any validation errors. Connor — can you confirm whether we need any extra onboarding fields for BrightArc in the portal?

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Manager, Procurement Ops | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai

Attachments: multi-po-template.csv (text/csv), BrightArc_sample_lines.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Accounting (BrightArc) <payables@brightarc.io>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 08:14:29 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Thanks Ava — that's clear and helpful. A couple of follow-ups: 

> On Sun, Jun 15, 2025 at 11:22 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> 
> > - Multi-PO: single PDF containing multiple PO numbers is acceptable, but you must include a simple CSV or XLSX that maps each invoice line (or subtotal) to the PO number. I attached multi-po-template.csv as an example.

1) For the CSV mapping, can you confirm if you need a per-line mapping (one line per invoice row) or is a per-subtotal mapping OK? Our exports can do either but default to subtotal groupings.

2) If we paste the OCR'd body in the email — will that be treated as an authoritative copy for routing, or does your AP team still require the CSV/XLSX for automated matching?

3) Our accounting system cannot produce PDF/A without a patch. We have an OCR service we can run before sending that produces an extracted CSV and a searchable PDF; would you accept both the searchable PDF + the extracted CSV PRIMARILY and keep the original scanned PDF as backup?

We'll start with two invoices next week; want to be sure we follow the path that minimizes manual rework.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

—Lucy

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 14:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Lucy — quick operational answers from AP below so you can run with your connector work: 

- Mapping granularity: per-line mapping is ideal because it avoids PO split ambiguity. If you must provide subtotals, include a clearly labelled column in the CSV called \"Subtotal_Group\" and list the PO for each group. Either option is fine as long as there's an explicit mapping.

- Email body OCR: searchable PDF + extracted CSV is best. If you do paste plain-text invoice lines into the email body, we will accept that as a fallback, but AP will still ask for the CSV for automated matching. Treat the pasted body as a convenience rather than a substitute.

- Acceptable file packaging: one PDF (searchable) + one CSV/XLSX mapping file. If you cannot produce searchable PDF, send: scanned PDF + extracted CSV + paste lines in email body. We'll mark the upload as \"manual review required.\"

- SUBJECT line: Please include all PO numbers as Ava suggested. Example subject we prefer: \"Invoice BrightArc — INV: 20250614 — PO: 1234,1250\"

If you want, send the two invoices to payables@redwood.ai and mark them \"portal fallback\" in the subject; I'll prioritize those for manual review to reduce processing lag while your portal connector is pending.

Marissa Cole

Subject: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks for starting the BrightArc vendor onboarding. A couple of practical questions from AP as we prepare our first invoice: 

1) Our system generates one PDF per invoice but we often need to charge against multiple Redwood POs on the same invoice (separate PO numbers for different teams). Is it acceptable to send a single PDF that contains multiple PO numbers and line items, or do you require one invoice per PO?

2) File naming — would you prefer the PDF subject line and filename to include the primary PO only, or all PO numbers? Example filename we currently use: BrightArc_Invoice_20250614.pdf.

3) Portal vs email: we prefer to submit via SFTP/portal but need a fallback to email while our connector is approved. If we email the invoice, what is your expected acknowledgement window (how quickly will you confirm receipt and expected payment date)?

4) OCR/searchability: our billing engine outputs scanned PDFs that are not searchable. Do you accept a scanned PDF plus a pasted plain-text invoice body in the email as an OCR fallback, or must the PDF itself be text-searchable (PDF/A)?

Attaching a sample invoice so you can see our layout (BrightArc_Inv_20250614.pdf). Happy to adapt — we just want to align before we submit several invoices next week.

Thanks,

Lucy Park
Finance Operations Lead, BrightArc
lucy@brightarc.io
+1 415-555-0182

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

Date: Sun, 15 Jun 2025 11:22:03 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Hi Lucy — great to meet you, and welcome. Short answers first, then some specifics and an attached template that should make multi-PO mapping straightforward.

Summary:
- Portal (preferred): please upload invoices to our Vendor Portal when possible. Portal uploads get an automated acknowledgement within 48 hours and are routed to AP for PO matching.
- Email (fallback): we will accept invoices sent to payables@redwood.ai while you finish connector work, but expect manual processing and a standard acknowledgement timeline of up to 3 business days.
- Multi-PO: single PDF containing multiple PO numbers is acceptable, but you must include a simple CSV or XLSX that maps each invoice line (or subtotal) to the PO number. I attached multi-po-template.csv as an example.
- File naming: include all PO numbers in the email Subject and in the filename where feasible (e.g., BrightArc_INV_20250614_PO1234_PO1250.pdf). If the filename would be too long, include primary PO in the filename and list all POs in the email subject and csv mapping.
- OCR fallback: preferred is a searchable PDF (PDF/A). If you cannot produce that, please paste the line-item table in the email body and attach a CSV/XLSX mapping. That combination will let our OCR/ingest team avoid manual re-keying.

Attached: multi-po-template.csv (CSV showing line->PO mapping) and BrightArc_sample_lines.xlsx (example of how we'd like line items exported).

Couple of operational notes:
- When emailing, put the SUBJECT as: Invoice BrightArc — PO: 1234,1250 — INV: 20250614 -> this helps automated routing.
- For portal uploads, the portal will return an acknowledgement ID and ETA; keep that acknowledgement ID for follow-up.

I'll loop in Marissa (AP) for any special payment-term exceptions; Neha handles vendor setup if you hit any validation errors. Connor — can you confirm whether we need any extra onboarding fields for BrightArc in the portal?

Best,
Ava

Proposed MAP & sequencing for IllumiHealth pilot
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Moore <alex.moore@illumihealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed MAP & sequencing for IllumiHealth pilot

Alex — attaching a quick mutual action plan (MAP_IllumiHealth_v1.xlsx) that lays out the pilot evaluation milestones, gating criteria, and our recommended legal/procurement sequencing. High-level asks:

- Goal: 30-day technical evaluation (baseline accuracy + latency) followed by a 90-day pilot if metrics meet the acceptance thresholds.
- Dependencies we flagged: MSA signature OR approved interim terms, DPA execution, order form for pilot capacity (or temporary internal PO).
- Security evidence: SOC 2 report + data handling appendix to be shared with your security team.

Proposed sequencing (we can flex):
1) DPA signed (DocuSign) — allows secure test data exchange
2) Limited sandbox provisioned (no PHI) — start technical eval week 0
3) MSA negotiation in parallel; aim to execute within 3 weeks
4) Order form / PO for pilot capacity before production ramp

If procurement needs a different ribbon to run in order (e.g., MSA first), we can accommodate — calling this out so legal/procurement can pick the path they prefer.

Next steps: can you share procurement SLAs / preferred contract owner so we sync legal on prioritization? I can also route a DPA to DocuSign this week to unblock the sandbox.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Account Executive | Redwood Inference
attachments: MAP_IllumiHealth_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Alex Moore <alex.moore@illumihealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@illumihealth.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Jun 2025 11:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP & sequencing for IllumiHealth pilot

Ben — thanks for this and the clear plan. A couple of constraints from our side that will affect sequencing:

- Our legal team typically requires an executed MSA before we approve a vendor to run anything on our network. They may accept a signed DPA earlier for data transfer, but their preference is MSA-first for outward-facing vendors.
- Procurement has a standard PO lead time of 3–4 weeks (internal approvals + budget release).
- We need access to your SOC 2 Type II report and an inventory of subprocessors before we can complete vendor risk assessment.

If we can get a DPA and the SOC 2 to start a constrained sandbox (no production data, isolated tenant), procurement may be willing to fast-track the PO once legal signals green. Otherwise, the MSA-first route will push the evaluation start by ~3 weeks.

I'll loop in the procurement owner, but for now please send the SOC 2 and any subprocessor docs. Can you also share a DocuSign DPA link so our legal can review in parallel?

Best,
Alex
Alex Moore | Head of Data Platforms | IllumiHealth
procurement@illumihealth.com


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Moore <alex.moore@illumihealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Jun 2025 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed MAP & sequencing for IllumiHealth pilot

title: Demo recap + proposed POC plan and acceptance checklist - KestrelTech
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <elena.ortiz@kestreltech.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Jun 2025 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Demo recap + proposed POC plan and acceptance checklist - KestrelTech

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and a compact POC proposal so we can confirm next steps with engineering and procurement.

Call summary (short)
- Goal: validate Redwood inference for KestrelTech’s document-extraction pipeline (invoice/PO normalization) at 99% label-level accuracy and sub-150ms median token latency for inference at 4 concurrent streams.
- Primary success criteria:
  1) Extraction F1 >= 0.92 on our shared sample (see sample-workload-requests.csv)
  2) End-to-end 90th percentile latency < 220ms; median < 150ms for production-ish batch sizes
  3) Token cost amortized to target ~$0.003 per 1k tokens via batching/kv-cache during POC
- Stakeholders: Elena (Kestrel data lead), Arjun (IT integration), procurement (TBD)

Proposed POC (3-week compact sprint) — high level- Week 0 (pre-flight): exchange sample payloads + access method, sign MSA addendum (if needed).- Week 1 (ingest + baseline): Redwood runs baseline inference on provided samples and shares a short report with accuracy & latency metrics.
- Week 2 (optimizations): apply batching/cache/quantization profile; iterate with Kestrel on prompt templates and mapping rules.
- Week 3 (handoff + runbook): final run results, simple runbook for production handover, and a short exec summary for procurement.

Owners (Redwood): Soojin (SE) — baseline & perf tuning; Marissa (CS) — success criteria, acceptance testing; Aditya (AE) — project coordination + procurement point.

Attachments and next steps- Attached: Kestrel_POC_MAP_v1.pdf (mutual action plan stub) and sample-workload-requests.csv (example payloads).- Can you confirm Arjun’s email (for IT integration) and whether procurement wants a formal SOW or a brief addendum?
- If the timeline looks good, we’ll target a POC kickoff the week of Jun 23 and share the baseline report by Jun 30.

Thanks — short and intentional here so we can lock dates.

— Aditya
Aditya Rao | Account Executive
Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0163


From: Elena Ortiz <elena.ortiz@kestreltech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Arjun Patel <arjun.patel@kestreltech.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Jun 2025 09:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Demo recap + proposed POC plan and acceptance checklist - KestrelTech

Hi Aditya —

Appreciate the concise plan. A few clarifications from our side so we’re all aligned:

1) Success criteria: the extraction F1 target is OK but we need per-field thresholds for invoice number, total amount and tax lines — can your SE provide a quick suggested acceptance table for those fields?
2) Latency: the 150ms median target is acceptable but please include a test with our 2x burst scenario (we have spikes up to 12 concurrent streams for payment runs).
3) Data handling: we need confirmation that logs retained during the POC will be encrypted and removed after handoff — SOC2 controls were discussed but procurement will require a short note.

Arjun is on copy; he’ll confirm the integration endpoint (we prefer an s3 presigned ingest for the POC). Procurement (I’ve looped them after this thread) will advise on contracting format — likely an addendum rather than full SOW given the short duration.

Dates: Jun 23 kickoff looks feasible for us, but we need two weeks’ notice for environment prep.

Can Soojin send the per-field acceptance table and a short runbook excerpt for the burst test plan?

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Ortiz
Head of Data & Automation | KestrelTech
elena.ortiz@kestreltech.com

title: Riverview - tenant-scoped forensic retention exception (SIG/CAIQ evidence)
preview:
From: Sophie Marks <sophie.marks@riverviewhealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@riverviewhealth.com
Date: Sat, 07 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG follow-up — request to retain tenant debug traces for 180 days

Hi Aditya,

Thank you again for the demo yesterday. Riverview's security team is working through the SIG and CAIQ attachments you shared. One item has come up that I wanted to surface early:

- Our incident response playbook requires up to 180 days of full debug traces for certain regulated endpoints (PCI-adjacent workflows).
- Redwood's default tenant-level ephemeral debug TTL is 30 days.

Can you confirm whether Riverview can request a tenant-scoped exception to preserve full debug traces for a named tenant (tenant id: riverview-tenant-42) for 180 days, and what evidence you would provide in SIG/CAIQ to support that exception (e.g., encryption-at-rest, access logging, proof-of-delete process, change control)?

We need enough artifactation for our SOC auditors and an initial statement of compensating controls to include in our SIG submission. If a formal exception requires an addendum or SOW line item, please flag that as well.

Appreciate a quick read — we are on a tight audit timeline.

Best,
Sophie
Sophie Marks
Senior InfoSec Manager, Riverview Health
Sophie.Marks@riverviewhealth.com

---
Attachments: Riverview_SIG_Template_v3.xlsx


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Marks <sophie.marks@riverviewhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Jun 2025 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up — request to retain tenant debug traces for 180 days

Hi Sophie,

Thanks for the clear summary and the SIG template — we can support a tenant-scoped carveout for forensic/debug traces, with some caveats:

1) We can extend debug trace retention for an identified tenant to 180 days, stored in an encrypted, access-controlled bucket that is logically scoped to that tenant.
2) Access will be limited to a named ops on-call and a small set of authorized engineers; all access will be logged and immutable audit trails will be available for the requested timeframe.
3) We will document the proof-of-delete procedure and provide a sample proof artifact (hash plus timestamp) after deletion.
4) Exceptions that increase storage/operational cost will be treated as a billing add-on under the Dedicated/Private contract or as an enterprise addendum for hosted customers.

I'm attaching a short design doc and the types of artifacts we can provide for SIG/CAIQ (control IDs, screenshots of audit logs, sample proof-of-delete). Ben and Neha — can you review the proposed artifact list and confirm whether we need legal signoff on the carveout wording?

Sincerely,
Aditya Rao
Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference

title: SLA negotiation — operational exception request and monthly credit proposal
preview:
From: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@edgewave.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Edgewave Procurement <procurement@edgewave.io>
Date: 2025-06-05T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: SLA baseline request — operational exception for batch workloads

Hi Avery,

Thanks for the call this morning. To summarize Edgewave's position for the contract team: because a significant portion of our traffic will be large batched inference jobs during nightly windows, we need an exception to the standard 99.95% uptime baseline — operationally those jobs can trigger transient host scaling events and scheduled reboots that briefly impact availability.

Specifically we're asking for:
- an allowance that the hosted uptime target be 99.90% for the first 6 months of the contract for the \"batching\" routes only;
- a capped monthly credit at 5% of committed spend for availability-related credits (rather than uncapped per-incident credits);
- explicit maintenance carveouts for pre-scheduled cluster scaling or kernel updates when Redwood provides 48h notice.

I attached a short worksheet showing our projected monthly usage and why the capped-credit approach is important for our finance team.

Can you confirm whether this is something Redwood can accept as an exception, or propose an alternate guardrail (e.g., temporary reduced uptime baseline with a phased uplift)?

Thanks,
Tom
Procurement, Edgewave

Attachments: monthly_credit_calc.xlsx (spreadsheet, 42KB)

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@edgewave.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-06-05T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SLA baseline request — operational exception for batch workloads

Tom — thanks for the clear summary and the worksheet.

Short answer: we can explore a scoped exception for batch-only routes, but we need clear operational guardrails so that the exception doesn't erode availability for customer-facing low-latency routes. A few thoughts from our side:

- Scope: make the exception route-specific (service name or endpoint path) and timebound (first 6 months, with metrics review at month 4).
- Uptime baseline: 99.90% for the named batch routes during the trial window is reasonable if paired with a small, fixed credit cap and tighter maintenance notice.
- Credit cap: instead of a flat 5% of spend, Redwood prefers a monthly credit cap expressed as a dollar/credit cap tied to the committed monthly spend (Redwood can propose a table). We can run their worksheet through our modeling.
- Maintenance carveouts: 48h notice is acceptable for planned kernel/driver work; we need to exclude emergency remediation and provider-imposed outages.

I'm looping in Marissa (contracts) and Ben (account eng) to draft a short redline. We'll attach a proposed language and a worked example of the monthly cap so procurement can confirm.

Can you share the exact route names or service tags you plan to use for batched jobs? That will speed the route-level scoping.

Best,
Avery
Senior Solutions, Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@edgewave.io>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-06-06T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SLA baseline request — operational exception for batch workloads

Tom — following up with proposed contract language (short excerpt below) and a worked credit example. I'll attach a formal redline in Word for your legal team.

--- excerpt start ---
Section X.y (Batching routes exception)
For the subset of endpoints explicitly identified in Schedule A (\"Batch Routes\"), Provider will apply a temporary uptime baseline of 99.90% for the first six (6) months following production cutover. During this period:
(a) Monthly Service Credits for availability deviations related to Batch Routes will be capped at the lesser of (i) five percent (5%) of Customer's committed monthly Platform Fees, or (ii) $25,000 per month.
(b) Planned maintenance for infrastructure and driver updates affecting Batch Routes shall be excluded from uptime calculations when Provider provides no less than forty-eight (48) hours' prior written notice. Emergency maintenance required to mitigate security or stability incidents will not be excluded.
(c) At month four (4) Provider and Customer will jointly review operational metrics and may agree to an uplift schedule to standard baseline (99.95%) based on adherence to operational runbooks and incident frequency metrics.
--- excerpt end ---

Worked example included in the attached spreadsheet shows how the cap maps to your worksheet. If the $25k cap is too high/low, we can adjust the floor/ceiling.

Can you confirm the Schedule A route names today? If you need help enumerating them, Ben can run a short inventory with your engineering team.

Regards,
Marissa
Sr. Contracts Manager, Redwood Inference

Attachments: SLA_Redline_Edgewave_v1.docx (redline, 18KB); monthly_credit_calc.xlsx (worked example, 42KB)

From: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@edgewave.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-06-06T13:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SLA baseline request — operational exception for batch workloads

Marissa, Avery — thanks for the draft language, that helps a lot. Two quick points from legal/finance:

1) The 5%-of-commitment cap maps well to our risk model, but we need the $ cap to be a sliding formula tied to committed spend (e.g., min($10k, 2% of monthly commit) — max($25k, 6% of monthly commit)). Can you propose a simple formula?
2) On carveouts: emergency maintenance carveouts are fair; we do want to preserve a right to a modest remediation credit if Redwood schedules recurring planned maintenance at the same timezone window more than twice in a 30-day period.

We can provide the list of batch route tags by EOD — we use route tags: /v1/batch-infer, /v1/embeddings-bulk, and a named job queue 'edgewave-batch'.

-Tom

Subject: SLA baseline request — operational exception for batch workloads

Hi Avery,

Thanks for the call this morning. To summarize Edgewave's position for the contract team: because a significant portion of our traffic will be large batched inference jobs during nightly windows, we need an exception to the standard 99.95% uptime baseline — operationally those jobs can trigger transient host scaling events and scheduled reboots that briefly impact availability.

Specifically we're asking for:
- an allowance that the hosted uptime target be 99.90% for the first 6 months of the contract for the \"batching\" routes only;
- a capped monthly credit at 5% of committed spend for availability-related credits (rather than uncapped per-incident credits);
- explicit maintenance carveouts for pre-scheduled cluster scaling or kernel updates when Redwood provides 48h notice.

I attached a short worksheet showing our projected monthly usage and why the capped-credit approach is important for our finance team.

Can you confirm whether this is something Redwood can accept as an exception, or propose an alternate guardrail (e.g., temporary reduced uptime baseline with a phased uplift)?

Thanks,
Tom
Procurement, Edgewave

Tom — thanks for the clear summary and the worksheet.

Short answer: we can explore a scoped exception for batch-only routes, but we need clear operational guardrails so that the exception doesn't erode availability for customer-facing low-latency routes. A few thoughts from our side:

- Scope: make the exception route-specific (service name or endpoint path) and timebound (first 6 months, with metrics review at month 4).
- Uptime baseline: 99.90% for the named batch routes during the trial window is reasonable if paired with a small, fixed credit cap and tighter maintenance notice.
- Credit cap: instead of a flat 5% of spend, Redwood prefers a monthly credit cap expressed as a dollar/credit cap tied to the committed monthly spend (Redwood can propose a table). We can run their worksheet through our modeling.
- Maintenance carveouts: 48h notice is acceptable for planned kernel/driver work; we need to exclude emergency remediation and provider-imposed outages.

I'm looping in Marissa (contracts) and Ben (account eng) to draft a short redline. We'll attach a proposed language and a worked example of the monthly cap so procurement can confirm.

Can you share the exact route names or service tags you plan to use for batched jobs? That will speed the route-level scoping.

Best,
Avery
Senior Solutions, Redwood Inference

Section X.y (Batching routes exception)
For the subset of endpoints explicitly identified in Schedule A (\"Batch Routes\"), Provider will apply a temporary uptime baseline of 99.90% for the first six (6) months following production cutover. During this period:
(a) Monthly Service Credits for availability deviations related to Batch Routes will be capped at the lesser of (i) five percent (5%) of Customer's committed monthly Platform Fees, or (ii) $25,000 per month.
(b) Planned maintenance for infrastructure and driver updates affecting Batch Routes shall be excluded from uptime calculations when Provider provides no less than forty-eight (48) hours' prior written notice. Emergency maintenance required to mitigate security or stability incidents will not be excluded.
(c) At month four (4) Provider and Customer will jointly review operational metrics and may agree to an uplift schedule to standard baseline (99.95%) based on adherence to operational runbooks and incident frequency metrics.
--- excerpt end ---

Aditya,

Thanks for sending the draft. Our legal team reviewed the MSA and Order Form and flagged three areas that are blocking procurement: output ownership (Section 3.2), training/data-use (Section 7.1), and the feedback/telemetry license (Section 9).

High level asks from DotMetrics:
- Customer retains title to any Customer Output generated by the service (no assignment to vendor).
- No use of Customer content or outputs for training models without explicit opt-in (we need an express \"training opt-in\" rather than implicit rights).
- Any feedback or telemetry Redwood uses must be aggregated, de-identified, and limited to improving the service; cannot be used for model retraining that produces models used by others unless we agree a separate addendum.

We attached our redlines (DotMetrics_MSA_redlines_20250605.pdf). Procurement also needs a proposed tag for a PO and expects a DocuSign-ready Order Form. Can you route this to your legal and provide a short point-by-point response?

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@dotmetrics.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Jun 2025 13:18:00 -0700
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — proposed clause language and fallback

Team / Samir — attaching proposed clause text (also pasted below) to address DotMetrics' concerns while protecting service operation. I tried to keep changes narrow and operational.

Key points:
- Title vs. License: customer keeps title to Customer Output (we admit they \"own\" the output). Redwood requests a limited license back only to the extent needed to provide the service to that customer and to perform troubleshooting/support. No assignment of title.
- Training / Model Updates: For Hosted accounts, default is: Redwood will NOT use Customer Content or Customer Output to retrain foundation models for third-party use unless Customer elects the \"training opt-in\". For Private (VPC / on-prem) deployments, Redwood will not use any Customer Content or Output for training.
- Feedback & Telemetry: Redwood may collect and use Feedback (as defined) solely in aggregated and de-identified form to improve the Redwood service. We propose language that: (a) prohibits use of Customer-specific Content for training without opt-in, (b) allows aggregated telemetry for internal product improvement, (c) limits retention of de-identified telemetry for retraining use to 12 months unless an express addendum is signed.

Proposed patch (plain text for easy redline):
\"Customer retains title to Customer Output. Subject to Customer's ownership of Customer Output, Customer grants Redwood a limited, non-exclusive, revocable license to store, reproduce, and display Customer Output solely to provide the Services, to perform support and troubleshooting, and to comply with law.

Redwood shall not use Customer Content or Customer Output to train or improve models that are used to provide services to other customers unless Customer has separately provided explicit written opt-in for such training. For Hosted Services, Customer may enable an opt-in toggle in the Console to permit use of anonymized and aggregated data for model improvements; absent affirmative opt-in, Redwood will not include Customer Content or Output in training corpora. For Private Deployments, no Customer Content or Output will be used for training absent a signed addendum.

Redwood may collect Feedback and operational telemetry in de-identified and aggregated form and may use such aggregated data to improve Redwood offerings; provided that Redwood will not re-identify or attempt to reverse engineer Customer Content. Use of aggregated telemetry for any model retraining that would result in models offered to third parties requires a separate written agreement. Redwood agrees to a 12-month limitation on using de-identified telemetry for retraining unless Customer agrees to a longer period in writing.\"

Ask: Samir, is the 12-month window acceptable as a starting point? If not, propose a term. Also note the opt-in toggle will be a console feature for Hosted accounts; ops will capture the choice in our contract metadata.

I'll incorporate feedback and produce a redline-ready doc for DocuSign once we settle the high level.

— Ben
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
---

- Suggest we include an early ramp smoke test (small traffic window) before the pilot acceptance test.
- Recommend we enable prefix caching and 512-token chunking in the first 30 days to reduce cost and stabilize latency.
