title: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Jordan + Evelyn —

We’re kicking off the public benchmark report focused on production inference economics (latency/$) across a curated model + region set. Before we get deep into runs + content, I want to align on (1) goals, (2) timeline, and (3) executive review checkpoints so we don’t drift and so Legal is looped at the right moments.

Proposed goals (what we’re optimizing for):
- Credible, technically defensible report + interactive page that engineers can trust and share.
- Clear story for Dedicated + Private pipeline (regional consistency + predictable throughput + operational controls), without over-claiming.
- Reproducible methodology artifacts retained internally (manifests/logs) and a publishable “how we measured” section.

What we will NOT do:
- No “best overall” / absolute claims. No cherry-picked prompts. No “X% faster than <competitor>” unless Legal signs off on substantiation + caveats.

Timeline (high level):
- Week 1–2 (Jun 3–Jun 14): methodology lock + instrumentation, model/region list locked, review checklist.
- Week 3–5 (Jun 15–Jul 5): region sweeps + validation, draft narrative + charts.
- Week 6–7 (Jul 6–Jul 19): internal reviews (Eng/PM/Sales/Legal/Security), finalize PDF + web.
- Week 8 (Jul 20–Jul 26): launch + webinar + post-launch monitoring.

Exec checkpoints I’m proposing:
1) Methodology sign-off (what counts as “fair”) — Jun 12/13
2) “So what?” / key findings review (high-level chart review) — week of Jun 30
3) Final claims + messaging sign-off (post-Legal) — week of Jul 7
4) Go/no-go readiness (launch checklist: web, PDF, enablement, support) — week of Jul 14

Asks:
- Jordan: can you confirm you’re comfortable with the scope and the above checkpoints? Anything you need specifically for Sales leadership enablement timing?
- Evelyn: would appreciate your view on how aggressive we should be on any competitor context (we’ll keep caveats prominent), and what an “executive-level” substantiation threshold should look like.

Attachments:
- Redwood_Benchmark_Report_Exec_OnePager_v0.3.pdf
- Benchmark_Report_Timeline_v0.2.xlsx

Thanks —
Camila

—
Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Works for me. I like the explicit “what we will not do” section — we should keep that tight in enablement too.

Two notes/asks from the revenue side:
1) Dedicated/Private angle: I want the takeaways to map cleanly to a sales motion (Hosted = get started fast; Dedicated = predictable throughput + isolation; Private = regulated + data residency). If the report is purely “hosted API latency/$”, we need a bridge in the narrative.
2) Competitive context: I’m fine with “contextual baselines” but only if we can explain it in one breath and point to methodology. Otherwise, we should lead with Redwood-only numbers + reproducibility.

Enablement timing: ideally we have a one-slide summary + top 5 FAQs by end of week of Jul 7 so Sales can start using it in outbound immediately.

If you can get the methodology sign-off on the calendar by next week, I’ll join.

Jordan

—
Jordan Blake
CRO, Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 2:21 PM PT, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 2:21 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Agree on the bridge. We’ll make sure the report includes a short section on “How to interpret the benchmark for your deployment mode” (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private) and we’ll also ensure the landing page CTAs route accordingly.

Re: competitor context — aligned. Default is Redwood-only. If we include any competitor baselines, it’ll be:
- clearly labeled as “context,”
- method + configs disclosed at a high level,
- strict caveats (variance, region differences, workload mismatch),
- and cleared by Legal.

Soojin — can you draft the Sales/SE asset timeline (one-slide + FAQ + talk track) to match Jordan’s Jul 7 target? We’ll keep it tied to the same “disallowed claims” list.

Evelyn — would still love your guidance on substantiation threshold / anything you’ve seen go sideways with benchmark launches.

I’ll have my assistant send holds for:
- Jun 13: methodology sign-off
- Jul 1: findings preview
- Jul 9: final claims/messaging sign-off
- Jul 15: go/no-go

Camila


On Thu, Jun 5, 2025 at 7:26 AM PT, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com> wrote:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jun 5, 2025 at 7:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

This is the right structure.

Substantiation threshold (what I’d treat as “exec-safe”):
- You can trace every headline number back to a run manifest and a retained raw log set.
- You can explain the workload in plain English (prefill vs decode, concurrency, cache policy) and why it maps to production.
- You have an explicit statement of what the benchmark does NOT represent (e.g., “this is not a universal ranking across all workloads”).
- For any comparisons: assume scrutiny. If Legal is not comfortable, don’t do it. It’s better to win on transparency.

Also: plan for external critique on “fairness.” Have a short prepared response that points to methodology + caveats, and be ready to share enough detail for a third party to reproduce (even if they won’t).

One last thought: if the interactive page is the main entry point, ensure the disclaimers are unmissable (not only in the PDF footnotes) and that export includes the same caveats.

Happy to join the methodology and final claims checkpoints.

Evelyn

—
Evelyn Hart
Operating Partner, Northstar VC

title: Escalation: Rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage for Redwood Private (Cascade Financial Group)
preview:
From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@cascadefg.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Becker <tom.becker@cascadefg.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Redwood Private)

Markus,

We’re preparing our next Redwood Private upgrade in our VPC environment and need written clarity on two items before we’ll schedule the maintenance window:

1) Rollback guarantees: if the upgrade fails (including DB migration issues), can we roll back to the prior version and what is the expected time to restore service? We need something more concrete than “best effort.”

2) Support coverage: we will be doing this in our standard maintenance window (Saturday 1:00–4:00am ET). We need to know if Redwood will have engineering/SRE coverage during the window, and what escalation path exists if rollback is required.

We’ve had issues with other vendors where “rollback” meant “rebuild the cluster,” which is not acceptable for us.

Please respond by EOD Thursday so we can confirm the window with our change advisory board.

Subject: Draft response to Cascade (rollback + support coverage)

All — draft below. Please reply with edits/approvals. If no objections by 2pm PT I’ll send.

---
Alyssa,

Thanks — understood on the need for operational clarity.

Rollback (supported procedure)
- For Redwood Private, we support in-place upgrades on the supported path (N-1 → N). As part of the upgrade workflow, Redwood requires a pre-upgrade backup/snapshot of the control-plane database and configuration state.
- If an upgrade fails (including during database migrations), the supported remediation is:
  1) stop/roll back the application components to the prior running version, and
  2) restore the control-plane database from the pre-upgrade snapshot (if migrations were applied and need to be reverted).
- In environments with pre-upgrade snapshots in place, service restoration is typically on the order of tens of minutes. Exact timing depends on the snapshot mechanism (managed DB PITR vs volume snapshot) and the size of the database.

Upgrade window support coverage
- We can staff your planned maintenance window (Sat 1:00–4:00am ET) with Redwood support + SRE escalation.
- Proposed plan: you share precheck outputs and the target version 48 hours prior; we open a live bridge at the start of the window; Redwood SRE/release escalation is available throughout until completion or rollback confirmation.

Auditability
- We can provide an upgrade report containing precheck results and timestamps for backup/upgrade steps. If required, we can also provide audit log entries that reflect upgrade start/stop and rollback invocation (version dependent).

If you’d like, we can also run a rollback drill in a non-production environment prior to the production change window.

---

Markus

From: Sophie Park <sophie.park@freshcart.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick demo request — embeddings for site search

Hi Avery,

I signed up via the self-serve flow and saw Redwood's embedding endpoints — would love a short demo to understand latency/cost for our product search use case. A little context:

- Company: FreshCart (online grocery marketplace)
- Use case: semantic product search + autosuggest (combine embeddings + reranking)
- Rough volume: ~1.2M queries/month currently, expect 3–4x peak season
- Peak concurrency: ~18–25 qps during promos
- Timeline: pilot in ~6 weeks, production by Q4 if performance/costs look good

Can you share availability for a 30 min call next week? Prefer mornings PST. I attached a small traffic export.

Thanks,
Sophie Park
Head of Search & Relevance
FreshCart

Attachment: traffic_matrix_may2025.csv (CSV, 24KB)

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Park <sophie.park@freshcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo request — embeddings for site search

Hi Sophie — great to meet you and thanks for the context + attachment.

Quick questions so we can tailor the session and bring the right folks:

1) Latency SLO: what p50/p95 target do you need for search responses (including reranking)?
2) Is the query workload latency-sensitive (customer-facing search) or batch/async (recommendations)?
3) Expected request payload size (tokens) for embedding + reranker steps?
4) Are there any constraints around data residency or on-prem requirements?

Tentative availability (PST):
- Tue 6/3 09:00–09:30
- Tue 6/3 11:00–11:30
- Wed 6/4 08:30–09:00

If none of those work, tell me a couple windows and I can send a calendar invite. I'll include Ben (solutions) on the call to go deeper on throughput/benchmarking.

Cheers,
Avery Johnson
SDR (Inbound) | Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0183

P.S. If you prefer to self-book: https://calendly.com/avery-johnson/30min

From: Sophie Park <sophie.park@freshcart.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 18:44:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo request — embeddings for site search

Thanks, Avery — answers inline below.

> 1) Latency SLO: what p50/p95 target do you need for search responses (including reranking)?
We aim for p50 < 120 ms and p95 < 400 ms for the full search + rerank path.

> 2) Is the query workload latency-sensitive (customer-facing search) or batch/async (recommendations)?
Primary is customer-facing search, autosuggest is latency-sensitive; recommendations are secondary.

> 3) Expected request payload size (tokens) for embedding + reranker steps?
Queries are short (2–12 tokens), product metadata paragraphs vary — reranker input ~200–350 tokens on average.

> 4) Are there any constraints around data residency or on-prem requirements?
No on-prem requirement; must be hosted in US regions only.

Availability: Tue 6/3 09:00 PST works best. If possible, include a short agenda covering: 1) latency/cost tradeoffs, 2) quick throughput benchmarks, 3) next steps for a pilot.

I've attached a compact traffic export and a sample query set.

Thanks — looking forward.
Sophie

Attachment: traffic_matrix_may2025.csv (CSV, 24KB)

2) Is the query workload latency-sensitive (customer-facing search) or batch/async (recommendations)?
3) Expected request payload size (tokens) for embedding + reranker steps?
4) Are there any constraints around data residency or on-prem requirements?

Tentative availability (PST):
- Tue 6/3 09:00–09:30
- Tue 6/3 11:00–11:30
- Wed 6/4 08:30–09:00

If none of those work, tell me a couple windows and I can send a calendar invite. I'll include Ben (solutions) on the call to go deeper on throughput/benchmarking.

Cheers,
Avery Johnson
SDR (Inbound) | Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0183

P.S. If you prefer to self-book: https://calendly.com/avery-johnson/30min

From: Sophie Park <sophie.park@freshcart.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 18:44:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo request — embeddings for site search

Thanks, Avery — answers inline below.

> 1) Latency SLO: what p50/p95 target do you need for search responses (including reranking)?
We aim for p50 < 120 ms and p95 < 400 ms for the full search + rerank path.

> 2) Is the query workload latency-sensitive (customer-facing search) or batch/async (recommendations)?
Primary is customer-facing search, autosuggest is latency-sensitive; recommendations are secondary.

> 3) Expected request payload size (tokens) for embedding + reranker steps?
Queries are short (2–12 tokens), product metadata paragraphs vary — reranker input ~200–350 tokens on average.

> 4) Are there any constraints around data residency or on-prem requirements?
No on-prem requirement; must be hosted in US regions only.

Availability: Tue 6/3 09:00 PST works best. If possible, include a short agenda covering: 1) latency/cost tradeoffs, 2) quick throughput benchmarks, 3) next steps for a pilot.

I've attached a compact traffic export and a sample query set.

Thanks — looking forward.
Sophie

title: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Team —

Attaching an updated Q3 sizing workbook and Cobalt's usage assumptions (see attachments). They just signaled a POC + 6 month pilot with expected ramp to 250 concurrent inference slots at peak if the pilot goes well. Ben, this is the opportunity we discussed on last week's call.

Agenda for our quick huddle:
- Validate the conservative/likely/aggressive GPU slot numbers (tab: scenarios)
- Confirm whether we can cover a canary of 20 slots in Phoenix for June and 50 slots for July (region note below)
- Finance constraints: what monthly committed spend / minimum reserve would be required to keep latency SLOs intact?
- Infra: what lead time, and which SKU (A100x2 vs H100x1) would you recommend for the canary vs ramp?

Quick asks before the call: please review the \"infra_notes\" tab and add a one-line comment in the workbook if a number looks off.

Attachments: 2025Q3_pipeline_sizing_v2.xlsx (workbook), Cobalt_MVP_usage_estimates.pdf (customer assumptions)

—Camila
Director, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Thanks Camila — reviewed the workbook. Short notes:  

- My conservative/likely/aggressive splits line up with the tab but I think the \"likely\" curve underestimates pilot conversion. Suggested: pilot -> 20 slots (canary), month 2 -> 80, month 3 -> 180 for the 6 month ramp.
- Cobalt has asked for an SLA of 99.5 p95 latency. That pushes us to reserved capacity vs pure burst.
- Proposed commercial: 3-month reserved commit for canary (20 slots) to secure capacity + monthly auto-scale burst credits. Will need finance sign-off but this is our working ask when we present pricing.

I'll add a new column in the sheet with my proposed commit profile. Can we aim to finalize optics by EOD tomorrow so I can fold into the HT lead's commercial?

-Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 09:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
>
> Attaching an updated Q3 sizing workbook and Cobalt's usage assumptions...
>


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Following up on Ben's notes. Finance perspective: 

- We can support a 3-month reservation for the 20-slot canary if the ARR uplift projections in the workbook are met. Need explicit milestone-based credit terms (PO, signed SOW, payment within 30 days) to justify the short reservation.
- For a ramp to 180-250 slots we need a minimum 6- to 12-month commitment tier; otherwise cost of burst will exceed margin thresholds in month 3.
- Please add a cashflow projection (month-by-month) and I will run sensitivity for revenue recognition / deferred revenue impact.

I flagged a couple of assumptions in the sheet (row comments) where the customer forecast looks overly front-loaded. If they truly ramp slow, we should recommend a staged reserved schedule: 20 -> 80 -> 180 tied to signed milestones.

Laura
Director, Revenue Operations & Finance
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 10:04 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Camila — reviewed the workbook. Short notes: ...
>


From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 14:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Infra constraints and recommended plan: 

- Short answer: we can stand up a 20-slot canary in PHX within 10 business days if we route to dedicated A100x2 pools. H100 provisioning lead times currently sit at ~6 weeks in our supply queue — so if the customer insists on H100 for latency/Perf, plan accordingly.
- For the July ramp to 50-80 slots, we can use a mixed SKU approach: canary on A100x2 reserved, burst to H100x1 where latency-critical. This preserves cost while honoring 99.5 p95 target.
- Key risk: simultaneous close of two other enterprise deals in July (see capacity reservations sheet). If both convert, we hit 85% pool utilization in PHX and will require cross-region spilling to us-west-2 (extra egress and latency impact).

Action items for infra:
1) Reserve 20 A100x2 slots in PHX (can set a 30-day hold)
2) Pre-book 50 H100x1 burst units for July (soft allocation)
3) Run a canary SLA test (5–10 synthetic clients) and capture p95 under load; send results to Camila and the Cobalt team.

-Jordan
Head of Infra Operations
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 11:03 AM Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Following up on Ben's notes. Finance perspective: ...
>


From: Ana Soto <ana.soto@cobalta.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 16:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Hi Camila / team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and for walking us through the workbook on the call today. A few confirmations from Cobalt:

- We agree to a 3-month reserved pilot for the 20-slot canary (PO will go to procurement next week).
- Cobalt requests the canary to be in PHX (lower RTT for our East Coast clients) and would like an option to convert to a 12-month commitment if KPIs (latency, throughput) are met by month 2.
- Procurement will need an expected pricing table + DocuSign SOW so we can calendar the PO. Can you share the DocuSign link and final pricing by EOD Monday?

Attachments: None (we will send PO pdf once procurement completes internal review)

Best,
Ana Soto
Head of ML Ops
Cobalt Analytics

title: Initial DPA intake — subprocessor chart & e-sign pathing
preview:
From: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@payarc.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Legal Ops <legalops@payarc.com>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: DPA request for evaluation + subprocessor list (PayArc)

Hi Ben,

We're kicking off the security and legal review for our planned integration with Redwood. Can you send over your standard DPA template and the current list of subprocessors (including locations and transfer mechanisms)? We've attached a short vendor questionnaire that our procurement/legal team uses to triage.

Also: what e-sign solution do you prefer? Our procurement team usually routes contracts via AdobeSign, but we can accept DocuSign if that's Redwood's default. Any preference (or blocking issues) on your side for AdobeSign?

Requested timeline: we'd like to have a signed DPA within two weeks if possible.

Thanks,
Maria Gonzalez
Head of Legal Operations
PayArc Payments
---
Attachment: vendor-questionnaire.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@payarc.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA request for evaluation + subprocessor list (PayArc)

Hi Maria — thanks for the clear ask and the questionnaire.

Short answers up front: we'll send our current DPA template and subprocessor registry for review. Redwood can execute via DocuSign by default; we can accommodate AdobeSign for enterprise customers but may need a short routing exception depending on your procurement toolchain. I'll loop in legal (Karthik) and your account lead (Laura) to align on timeline.

Action items:
- I'll attach the DPA template and a redline-ready copy (internal) for your review.
- Karthik, can you confirm if any clauses commonly get flagged for AdobeSign execution?
- Laura, please confirm the expected environment (prod us-west-2 or eu-west-1) so we can include accurate data flow notes.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: PayArc_DPA_Request.pdf (pdf)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@payarc.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 09:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA request for evaluation + subprocessor list (PayArc)

Hi all — attaching our editable DPA template (Redwood_DPA_v1_redline.pdf) and a short subprocessor summary CSV in the next message. A few notes from legal/privacy: 

1) Subprocessors: the attached registry includes current infra, telemetry, and analytics subprocessors. For transfers: primary infra instances are in us-west-2; where we rely on third-party analytics, standard EU-US SCCs or EU-US Data Transfer Framework provisions apply depending on the jurisdiction.

2) E-sign preference: DocuSign is our operational default and integrates with our contract lifecycle system. We can accept AdobeSign for this agreement, but our legal ops team will need to add a short execution addendum (1 paragraph) documenting an equivalent audit trail and retention. That addendum is non-substantive. If PayArc requires AdobeSign, please confirm and we'll prepare the addendum.

3) Common redlines: customers sometimes request deletion timing changes and additional logging detail. We're open to limited, reciprocal edits but ask for tracked redlines so we can run them across our standard set.

4) Timeline: two weeks is tight but feasible if we have the vendor questionnaire back with prioritized items and a single decision-maker for legal/procurement.

Karthik Iyer
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
Legal & Privacy
---
Attachment: Redwood_DPA_v1_redline.pdf (pdf)

From: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@payarc.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Legal Ops <legalops@payarc.com>, Procurement <procurement@payarc.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 08:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA request for evaluation + subprocessor list (PayArc)

Thanks Karthik — appreciate the clarity. A few quick clarifications from our side:

- We need the full subprocessor list CSV with locations and the legal basis for transfers (SCC vs other). Could you send that?
- PayArc will prefer AdobeSign for now — procurement has a standard workflow tied to Adobe. Please go ahead and include the execution addendum you mentioned.
- Procurement clarified that they only escalate redlines that change data residency or broaden subprocessor rights; please mark those sections.

We're compiling the vendor questionnaire and will return it by EOD tomorrow. If you can send the subprocessor CSV and addendum draft today, we can keep to the two-week target.

Best,
Maria
---
Attachment: vendor-questionnaire-filled_preview.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@payarc.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA request for evaluation + subprocessor list (PayArc)

Great — thanks everyone. Quick internal summary and next steps: 

- Karthik: please produce the subprocessor CSV (include data center regions and transfer mechanism column) and a one-paragraph AdobeSign execution addendum. Target: COB today.
- Laura: confirm environment — Maria, can you confirm whether PayArc will be connecting to a production instance in us-west-2? Laura, please prepare a short note on data flow (ingest, KV caching, logs retention) to include in the DPA appendix.
- Maria: we'll review your completed questionnaire tomorrow and flag any substantive items. If procurement insists on AdobeSign, we'll attach Karthik's addendum and proceed with AdobeSign envelope.

I'll follow up with a DocuSign/AdobeSign link once Karthik confirms the addendum is ready.

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: Orchid AI - SLA uptime anchoring and monthly remedy proposal
preview:
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t trigger credits.

Procurement also asked for: monthly remedy cap (we’d like a hard cap of 2% of monthly invoice) and a weekly operational report for the pilot period. I attached our redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf).

Can Redwood accept these pilot terms, or propose an alternative that achieves the same operational protection for Orchid?

Best,
Ethan
Ethan Ross
Head of Platform Ops, Orchid AI
ethan.ross@orchid.ai


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 10:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ethan — thanks for the clear note and the redlines. Short answers up front: we can accommodate a temporary pilot uptime anchor and define explicit batch windows as carveouts, but we have a few guardrails we need to propose (see below).

Proposed counterpoints:
- Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood can agree to a 98.0% provisional uptime floor for the first 90 days of the pilot, with an automatic reversion to 99.5% after successful validation. We’ll include objective pass/fail criteria tied to your traffic profile to avoid ambiguity.
- Batch latency carveouts: we can exclude scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) from credit calculations provided you flag those windows in advance and they are limited to repeating nightly maintenance/batch jobs (not ad-hoc production spikes).
- Monthly remedy cap: we typically cap monthly credits but not as low as 2% for pilot customers — that would materially limit remedy viability. Proposed alternative: cap monthly remedy to 5% of invoice for the pilot, with an opt-in to lower caps post-production if you require it.
- Operational reporting: we can provide a weekly pilot health digest (uptime/latency token-level breakdowns, incidents, and KV cache hit-rate) during onboarding.

I’m looping in Ben Carter (Sales) and Laura Bennett (Legal) to align on deal mechanics and a compact SLA amendment doc. Attached is a short counter (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) illustrating the language we'd like to use. If that looks reasonable we can finalize legal language and get a DocuSign link out this week.

Next steps: please confirm you’re comfortable with the 5% pilot cap alternative and the batch-window requirements, or propose a compromise. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ben/Laura — forwarding Ethan's redlines and my proposed counter. Key points for discussion:

- Pilot uptime anchor at 98.0% for 90 days is grantable. We should add explicit validation criteria: e.g., 7-day rolling p95/availability sample across their top-5 routes. If they fail to reach 99.5% after pilot, we escalate to a playbook rather than immediate credit clawbacks.
- Batch carveouts: OK to exclude nightly windows with the condition that Orchid provides a weekly schedule and we reserve right to reclassify repeated daytime spikes as production traffic.
- Remedy cap: Procurement wants 2%. I pushed back and suggested 5%. Laura, can you take a look at the counter doc and advise if we have room to accept 3% as a compromise during pilot? Also confirm whether we should add a clause limiting credit payouts to a maximum cumulative amount over the pilot term.

Quoting Ethan below for context.

-----Original Message-----
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Sent: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: ...


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ethan — thanks for reviewing and for the redlines. Laura and Ben reviewed and we propose the following formal counter for the pilot term: 

1) Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood will document a provisional uptime floor of 98.0% for the first 90 days starting on the go-live date. We’ll include objective validation criteria (7-day rolling availability sample across their top-5 routes) to confirm drift back to 99.5%. If Orchid meets validation, baseline reverts automatically; if not, we engage an operational remediation playbook (no automatic credit acceleration).

2) Latency carveouts: Scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) will be excluded from monthly credit calculations provided Orchid supplies a weekly schedule and the windows are limited to recurring batch jobs. Ad-hoc large jobs outside those windows will remain in scope.

3) Monthly remedy cap: For the pilot period only, Redwood proposes a 4% cap on monthly remedy (a midpoint between your 2% ask and our standard exposure). Payouts remain subject to incident validation and remediation offsets. After the pilot, we revert to our standard remedy cap unless mutually amended.

4) Reporting and cadence: Redwood will send a weekly pilot health digest (PDF) and host a weekly 30-min check-in during the 90-day pilot to review anomalies, trending, and any scheduling conflicts.

Attached: updated SLA amendment (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) and a marked copy of your redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf). If the 4% cap is acceptable, we’ll route the amended SLA to DocuSign and target signatures by end of week. If you prefer to discuss, I’m free Thu 10–12 PT.

Thanks and looking forward to getting the pilot started.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

title: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 8:14 AM PT
Subject: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks
Attachments: PLG_Signals_Pilot_Week1_Summary_2025-05-27.pdf (application/pdf)

Team — quick Week 1 readout for the NA PLG→sales signal pilot (Mon–Sun).

Cohort + volume
- Cohort: 212 NA self-serve companies (paid + high-usage trials) in the pilot list
- Signals generated (raw): 96
- Signals routed to HubSpot tasks (qualified after suppression/filters): 41
- Unique companies with ≥1 routed signal: 28
- Median time from signal to task creation: 19 min (p95: 2h 06m)

Early funnel outcomes (Week 1)
- First-touch completed (email or call logged): 17 / 28 (61%)
- Meetings booked: 6 / 28 (21%)
- “Interested / evaluating” (no meeting yet): 5 / 28 (18%)
- Disqualified as “too small / not a fit / wrong contact”: 7 / 28 (25%)

What worked
- Reliability pain signals are converting: repeated 429s + sustained token growth produced the highest meeting rate (4/9)
- Dedicated/Private intent (docs/security page sequences) led to good discovery calls when combined with usage scale (not by itself)

Noise / false positives (qualitative)
- AEs flagged ~8 tasks as “low signal” where intent was purely docs browsing (pricing + dedicated docs) with no corresponding usage or 429s.
- 2 instances where marketing nurture overlapped with AE outreach within 24h (tone felt “pile-on” to the customer). Camila/tyler team already tracking.
- One customer complaint came in re: outreach timing/assumptions (see SUP-3392) — appears tied to a docs-view-only signal that got through.

Proposed threshold + suppression adjustments (for Week 2)
1) Intent-only gating: require intent signals to be paired with at least one “hard” indicator (spend velocity OR sustained tokens OR repeated 429s) to route as a task.
   - Exception: security/compliance intent (SOC2/security FAQ/VPC docs) can route without usage if the account is paid.
2) Increase “sustained usage” cut line for NA pilot from 3-day avg ≥ 2.5M tokens/day → 4.0M tokens/day (keeps volume manageable).
3) 429s: require ≥ 3 distinct hours with 429s in a 48h window (vs current ≥2) to reduce one-off rate limit spikes.
4) Add a 7-day re-notification suppression per company per signal_type unless severity increases (to avoid multiple tasks for the same underlying issue).

Asks
- Stephanie: does the meeting rate look directionally right vs your team capacity? If we ship the above changes, we project ~20–25 routed tasks/week (down from 41).
- Camila: OK if we add a 72h marketing suppression window for accounts that get an AE task created?
- Nikhil/David: can we implement the gating + new cut lines by EOD Thu so it affects next Monday’s routing?

Details in the attached 1-pager + the pilot cohort sheet is updated.

Ben
Ben Carter
Growth, Redwood Inference


---

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 9:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

This is helpful — thanks.

On capacity: 41 tasks/week was a little hot given NA team load. The 20–25/week target feels right for a pilot (esp if we’re prioritizing higher-quality).

AE feedback (from Avery + 2 others):
- The best ones were “growth + pain” (spend velocity + 429s / latency complaints). Those should keep going.
- The weakest ones were “they read docs” with no context — it reads like we’re watching them too closely. So I strongly support your intent-only gating.

One request: can we make the task titles more explicit about why it routed? Some were just “PLG signal: intent” and the AE had to click around.

Stephanie


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 9:28 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

+1 to gating intent-only. Marketing has the same concern — we should avoid “vanity intent” driving human outreach.

Re: suppression, yes we can do a 72h pause on lifecycle emails for accounts with an AE task created, but we need the signal to flow into HubSpot in a way the workflow can check reliably.

David — can you confirm we have a single property we can key off of (e.g., last_plg_signal_task_created_at) and that it’s consistently populated?

Also re SUP-3392: if we’re changing routing rules, I’d like a short customer-facing line we can use if it comes up again (“we reached out based on service usage patterns; happy to pause and we can disable proactive outreach”). I can draft, but want Legal/CS aligned.

Camila


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 10:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

Good early signal.

Two things I want us to be crisp on:
1) Quality bar: we should optimize for customer trust first, pipeline second. I’m comfortable trading volume for fewer false positives.
2) Measurement: can we add a weekly breakdown of meeting-booked rate by signal type (reliability vs scale vs security/compliance intent vs team expansion)? That will make the iteration loop faster.

Also, for the 6 meetings booked — any early read on whether these are trending Dedicated vs Private? Even directional is fine.

Jordan


---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

Agree on quality > volume.

Answers + next steps:

Meeting-booked by signal type (Week 1)
- Reliability (429s / elevated error rate + growth): 4 meetings booked (highest)
- Scale-only (sustained tokens/spend velocity without errors): 1 meeting booked
- Security/compliance intent: 1 meeting booked (paid account; asked for SOC2 + audit logs)
- Team expansion (new workspaces/invite spikes): 0 meetings booked (AEs said it’s better suited to CS/PLG nurture unless paired with spend growth)

Dedicated vs Private (directional)
- Dedicated-leaning: 4 (performance/throughput, predictable capacity)
- Private-leaning: 1 (data residency + retention controls)
- TBD: 1 (early discovery)

Task title / clarity (Stephanie’s point)
- We can update the task template to include the top 2 contributing signals + the window, e.g. “PLG: 429s (3 hrs/48h) + spend +62% WoW”

Marketing suppression (Camila)
- David/Nikhil: if we can populate one canonical property (e.g., plg_last_task_created_at) on company, Camila can key the 72h pause workflow off that. Otherwise we can use task creation itself, but it’s less deterministic.

Implementation plan (proposed)
- Ship intent-only gating + increased thresholds by Thu EOD
- Add 7-day re-notification suppression by next Monday (unless severity increases)
- Add weekly “signal→meeting by type” table to the pilot update starting next Tuesday

CS alignment
- Jasmine: for the team-expansion signal type, we should likely route as a CS heads-up (existing customers) or keep as a non-task note (new self-serve) until we have better qualification.

I’ll update the working notes + ping Nikhil with the concrete thresholds.

Ben

   - Exception: security/compliance intent (SOC2/security FAQ/VPC docs) can route without usage if the account is paid.
2) Increase “sustained usage” cut line for NA pilot from 3-day avg ≥ 2.5M tokens/day → 4.0M tokens/day (keeps volume manageable).
3) 429s: require ≥ 3 distinct hours with 429s in a 48h window (vs current ≥2) to reduce one-off rate limit spikes.
4) Add a 7-day re-notification suppression per company per signal_type unless severity increases (to avoid multiple tasks for the same underlying issue).

Asks
- Stephanie: does the meeting rate look directionally right vs your team capacity? If we ship the above changes, we project ~20–25 routed tasks/week (down from 41).
- Camila: OK if we add a 72h marketing suppression window for accounts that get an AE task created?
- Nikhil/David: can we implement the gating + new cut lines by EOD Thu so it affects next Monday’s routing?

Details in the attached 1-pager + the pilot cohort sheet is updated.

Ben
Ben Carter
Growth, Redwood Inference

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 9:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

This is helpful — thanks.

On capacity: 41 tasks/week was a little hot given NA team load. The 20–25/week target feels right for a pilot (esp if we’re prioritizing higher-quality).

AE feedback (from Avery + 2 others):
- The best ones were “growth + pain” (spend velocity + 429s / latency complaints). Those should keep going.
- The weakest ones were “they read docs” with no context — it reads like we’re watching them too closely. So I strongly support your intent-only gating.

One request: can we make the task titles more explicit about why it routed? Some were just “PLG signal: intent” and the AE had to click around.

Stephanie

Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

Re: suppression, yes we can do a 72h pause on lifecycle emails for accounts with an AE task created, but we need the signal to flow into HubSpot in a way the workflow can check reliably.

Also re SUP-3392: if we’re changing routing rules, I’d like a short customer-facing line we can use if it comes up again (“we reached out based on service usage patterns; happy to pause and we can disable proactive outreach”). I can draft, but want Legal/CS aligned.

Camila

- Private-leaning: 1 (data residency + retention controls)
- TBD: 1 (early discovery)

Marketing suppression (Camila)
- David/Nikhil: if we can populate one canonical property (e.g., plg_last_task_created_at) on company, Camila can key the 72h pause workflow off that. Otherwise we can use task creation itself, but it’s less deterministic.

Implementation plan (proposed)
- Ship intent-only gating + increased thresholds by Thu EOD
- Add 7-day re-notification suppression by next Monday (unless severity increases)
- Add weekly “signal→meeting by type” table to the pilot update starting next Tuesday

CS alignment
- Jasmine: for the team-expansion signal type, we should likely route as a CS heads-up (existing customers) or keep as a non-task note (new self-serve) until we have better qualification.

title: Evaluation & close execution timeline — GPU burst allocation and lead times
preview:
From: Emma Stone <emma@luminahealth.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Carlos Ruiz <carlos@luminahealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@luminahealth.com>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation & capacity questions — Lumina needs lead times

Hi Avery —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Leadership asked for a short mutual action plan: we need to validate latency on our clinical inference workload, confirm expected cost per 1M tokens, and lock a short runway for a dedicated GPU pool if the eval hits our performance targets. Key constraints on our side: procurement windows close the week of June 16 and our security review needs SOC2 artifact access by June 4.

Can you sketch an evaluation timeline that lines up with those dates and give a sense of GPU pool sizing for a pilot (~50 concurrent users, avg 512 tokens response)? Also: what are lead times from commitment to live for a Private VPC deployment vs Redwood Dedicated?

If you can send a short MAP and a proposed close timeline, I’ll circulate internally.

Best,
Emma

Emma Stone
Head of ML Platforms | Lumina Health
emma@luminahealth.com
+1 415 555 0142

Attachments: none


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Stone <emma@luminahealth.com>
Cc: Carlos Ruiz <carlos@luminahealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@luminahealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 10:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation & capacity questions — Lumina needs lead times

Emma —

Appreciate the clarity on deadlines. I put together a concise MAP and a recommended sprint to align the eval with your procurement window. High-level summary below; I attached a one-page checklist and a proposed GPU sizing chart.

Proposed MAP (high level):
- Week 0 (May 26–30): Kickoff + test dataset exchange; Redwood provides private eval creds and baseline perf report.
- Week 1 (Jun 2–6): Controlled latency & throughput tests (50 concurrent sim), prefix caching validation, and cost projection run. Need SOC2 evidence for security team by Jun 4 — Ben, can you confirm which artifacts we’ll surface by then?
- Week 2 (Jun 9–13): Acceptance criteria run; if latency and token cost within agreed bounds, move to procurement handoff.
- Week of Jun 16: Procurement completes PO/commitment for Dedicated or Private.
- Provisioning timeline post-commit: Redwood Dedicated: ~5 business days to allocate reserved GPU pool; Private VPC: ~3–5 weeks depending on network provisioning and KMS/HSM integration.

GPU pool sizing (pilot):
- Baseline estimate: 4 x A100-80GB equiv (or 8 x A10 equiv) to support sustained 50 concurrent sessions at avg 512 tokens with headroom for bursts.
- Surge/burst option: add 2 pre-warmed nodes for peak days — we can schedule autoscale bursts (pay-as-you-go) if you want to avoid long-term commit for the burst capacity.

Next steps I suggest: sign off on the checklist I attached, confirm SOC2 artifact contact (Ben will share), and we’ll prepare a short SOW + DocuSign draft timed for procurement week. I included Laura from Solutions to coordinate onboarding.

I’m available for a 30-min sync tomorrow if that helps.

Thanks,
Avery

Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.ai | +1 650 555 0203

Attachments: lumina-eval-checklist.xlsx, proposed-gpu-sprint-chart.png


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emma Stone <emma@luminahealth.com>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 10:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation & capacity questions — Lumina needs lead times

Avery / team —

Quick internal note before we commit to any lead-time promises to Lumina:
- SOC2 artifacts: we can provide the SOC2 Type II report and the system description PDF by Jun 4. For audit logs and retention specifics, I’ll prepare an export template (should be ready by end of day May 28).
- Private VPC lead times: network requests tend to be the gating factor. If Lumina’s networking team can pre-provide firewall rules and a static IP block, we can compress to ~2.5–3 weeks. Otherwise plan on 4–5 weeks.
- GPU sizing: the 4 x A100 suggestion is reasonable. Operational note: for Dedicated we can reserve gpus within 5 business days post-PO if stock is available; if they need H100/other specialized SKU, add 7–10 business days.

Action for our side: Neha, can you validate inventory and whether we can offer 1-week SLA for pool allocation if the customer accepts the standard Dedicated SKU?

Ben

Ben Carter
Solutions Engineering Manager | Redwood Inference


From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 May 2025 11:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation & capacity questions — Lumina needs lead times

Ben / team —

Inventory check done: for Dedicated-standard (A100-80GB equiv) we have capacity to meet a 5-business-day allocation for up to 8 nodes this quarter. H100 stock is constrained; if Lumina requests H100s we should flag a 10-business-day procurement lead time.

Also note: for a Private VPC we must schedule an onboarding window with our infra team for KMS integration and routing validation — typical slot availability is 2x per week; earliest open slot I see is Jun 2–6 which is tight but workable if Lumina commits to initial kickoff this week.

I’ll prepare a short internal checklist (I’ll paste into the Drive link) for pre-provisioning items they need to complete to hit the 3-week Private timeline.

-Neha

To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>

- Do you require invoices via the Redwood vendor portal or is emailing accounts payable acceptable as a long-term option?
- If email is acceptable, what inbox should we use and what exact subject line / filename conventions do you need?

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 22 May 2025 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — Silverline invoices

Hi Jill — thanks for the packet and sample invoice.

Short answers:
- Preferred: vendor portal (single source of truth) — please use the onboarding link I've included below to create your account. We can onboard you same-day once the form is complete.
- Email fallback: acceptable for the first 30 days or for smaller one-off charges. Send pdf invoices to accounts-payable@redwoodinference.com with subject line: "INV | Silverline | PO#XXXXX | <YYYYMMDD>". File name should mirror subject.
- Minimal invoice fields: PO number (required for PO-matched orders), Invoice number, Invoice date, Remit-to address, Line-item description + qty + unit price, Subtotal + tax + total, Vendor Tax ID (W-9 on file). We can accept consolidated invoices monthly but require PO references per line.

Quick asks for ops: Laura, can you confirm PO-stamping rules and whether we allow partial-match? Also attaching our public onboarding form link above.

Onboarding form: https://redwoodinference.app/vendor-onboard/form/SL-12345

To: Jill Turner <jill.turner@silverline.co>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 23 May 2025 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — Silverline invoices

Hi Jill — welcome (and thanks Ben for the intro). A few operational bullets and examples:

- PO stamping: our system will stamp invoices that match a PO number exactly. We allow partial-match only for recurring services when "Subscription" is selected on the onboarding form. Otherwise, please include PO per line.
- No-PO invoices: use subject "INV | Silverline | NO_PO | <YYYYMMDD>" and include a short justification in the body; those route to a manual queue and can delay payment 7–10 days.
- Remittance: remit block on the invoice is sufficient. We do not require a separate remittance PDF.
- Paper checks: handled rarely. If requested, send originals to:
  Redwood Inference
  Attn: Accounts Payable
  1800 Market St, Suite 300
  San Francisco, CA 94103

Filename examples we accept:
- INV | Silverline | PO#12345 | 20250531.pdf
- INV | Silverline | NO_PO | 20250531.pdf

Exceptions: for credit memos please prefix subject with "CREDIT | Silverline | <credit-number>".

We'll proceed with onboarding once the form and W-9 are back. If you want me to fast-track the vendor code assignment, include your proposed payment terms (Net 30/Net 45) in the form and cc me.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
AP Manager
Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0133

title: Third-party claim escalation, SLA exceptions & MSA redline coordination
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@datapulse.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@datapulse.com
Date: 2025-05-18T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (attachments)

Ben —

We received a notice from a third party alleging patent infringement tied to outputs generated during a customer proof-of-concept. Our legal team would like to tighten the indemnity and notice language in the MSA/order form before we sign the final PO. Key asks from our side:

- Defense control: we prefer vendor (you) to defend, but only for claims that clearly relate to Redwood's model outputs rather than our data; otherwise we want a carve-out.
- Notice and cure windows: 15 days isn't enough; proposing 30 days for us to respond and supply evidence.
- Evidence preservation: we need a defined evidence request list and contact for triage.
- Cap/insurance: can you confirm insurance offsets and whether a cap will be relative to the order form value?

I attached a redline of your MSA (DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf) with the customer's proposed language. We need a response this week so procurement can finalize the PO.

Can you confirm who on your side will own the technical evidence collection and the escalation path?

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Counsel, DataPulse
elena.ruiz@datapulse.com

Attachments: DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf, evidence_packet_summary.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (help needed)

Team — forwarding DataPulse counsel's note. Summary of their asks below and files attached. We need legal + sales aligned before sending a formal redline back. Procurement is threatening to hold PO if we don't respond by Friday.

Customer asks:
- Limit defense control to claims \"clearly related\" to Redwood outputs; otherwise carve out
- Extend notice/cure to 30 days
- Confirm insurance offsets and whether cap tied to order form value
- Provide evidence request list and contact for triage

Immediate asks for Kimberly: can you propose a compromise that minimizes long-term liability but keeps the deal moving?
For Jordan: what's the deal economics — would we accept a cap equal to one year of committed revenue, or do we need a higher threshold?
Grace: can you own ops/engineer evidence liaison if we agree to any defense-control language that requires technical handover?

Attachments: DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf, evidence_packet_summary.pdf

-Ben

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@datapulse.com>
Date: Sun, May 18, 2025 at 9:03 AM
Subject: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (attachments)
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@datapulse.com

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T11:40:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (legal proposal)

Ben / Jordan / Grace —

Thanks for the heads up. I reviewed DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf and the customer's comments. Below is a proposed position that balances legal risk and commercial momentum.

High-level compromise (legal):
1) Defense control: Offer a hybrid approach — Redwood retains initial defense control only for third-party claims arising directly from model outputs that are not traceable to the customer's proprietary prompt/data stream. For claims where customer data or customer-provided models are the likely cause, we propose a pass-through requiring customer to assume defense. This avoids us taking on open-ended coverage for customer data issues.

2) Notice/cure window: Accept 30 days for non-emergency evidence collection, but retain a 5-business-day \"notification of material escalation\" requirement so we can trigger retention holds. Put explicit steps for evidence request (list of artifacts) and a named ops contact for technical handover.

3) Insurance / offsets & cap: Propose cap = greater of (a) total fees paid in prior 12 months under the order form, or (b) $250k, with insurance offset language preserved but limited so that we don't get double-reduced below a reasonable threshold. If DataPulse needs a lower cap, escalate to me for exec review.

4) Cooperation: Add mutual cooperation clause with specific timelines for document production (e.g., 7 business days for logs, 10 business days for models / checkpoints where feasible), and privilege carve-out language for privileged counsel communications.

5) Evidence custody: Grace — we will need an on-call evidence custodian (ops engineer) to accept secure artifact transfer if this proceeds. Can you confirm availability and an intake process?

I attached a tracked redline with these changes (orderform-amendment-draft.docx). If Sales accepts the cap proposal, legal is comfortable sending this back as a counter to the customer's redline. If Jordan says the cap kills the economics, tell me the threshold and I will prepare an exec memo for a limited exception.

-Kimberly

Attachments: orderform-amendment-draft.docx

On Sun, May 18, 2025 at 10:15 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — forwarding DataPulse counsel's note. Summary of their asks below and files attached. We need legal + sales aligned before sending a formal redline back. Procurement is threatening to hold PO if we don't respond by Friday.
>
> -Ben

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@datapulse.com>, contracts@datapulse.com
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:32:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (proposed compromise)

Elena —

Thanks for raising this and for the redline. Kimberly and I discussed and we've prepared a compromise we think addresses both parties' concerns while keeping the PO on track. Summary of our position (see attached counter-redline):

- Defense control: Redwood will accept defense for claims that independent technical analysis shows arise from Redwood model outputs (not originating from DataPulse proprietary inputs). For claims plausibly attributable to DataPulse inputs or configurations, DataPulse will retain responsibility. We will include an escalation step to neutral technical review in the event of a dispute.
- Notice & cure: We can accept a 30-day notice/cure window for the customer's remediation, but require an immediate material escalation notice (5 business days) to trigger retention of evidence.
- Cap and insurance offsets: Proposed cap = greater of prior 12 months' fees or $250k. Insurance offsets will apply but not below a minimum residual recovery of $50k. If DataPulse wants a lower cap tied strictly to the single order value, we'll need executive approval (Jordan — are you able to sign off or should I route?).
- Evidence handover: Grace will be the ops evidence custodian. Technical intake will follow the checklist in the attached doc.

Next steps:
1) Review attached counter-redline (orderform-amendment-draft.docx with tracked changes).
2) If acceptable, sign the amendment via DocuSign: https://docusign.net/link/orderform-amend-DP-0412
3) If DataPulse requests a lower cap, ask for business justification and Jordan please confirm whether revenue impact allows escalation without delaying the PO.

We tried to be constructive here — want to keep procurement moving and avoid an overbroad defense obligation on Redwood.

-Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: orderform-amendment-draft.docx, DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf

title: HealthCore: Tenant-scoped deletion & field-redaction options for PHI workloads
preview:
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Avery / Diego,

Thanks for taking this. HealthCore is evaluating Redwood for a pilot that will ingest clinical chat transcripts, structured vitals, and small document attachments that include PHI. Procurement asked me to capture a few specific requirements before we move to a security review: 

- Can Redwood support per-tenant retention policies (we need different windows per environment)?
- For PHI fields (patient name, MRN, SSN, clinical notes) we need a configurable maximum retention window and an automatic irreversible redaction option once the window expires.
- We require proof-of-deletion exports (auditable report) that we can store off-site for our auditors.
- Are there field-level redaction options (mask tokens / hashed pseudonymization) at ingest vs. deletion-time?
- Any limitations for attachments (PDFs/images) — can they also be redacted/removed on a per-tenant schedule?

We can share a filled questionnaire if that helps. Goal is to understand constraints (latency, costs, and whether this impacts availability SLAs).

Thanks — let me know if a quick call this week helps.

— Sarah
Sarah Kim
Director, Security & Compliance
HealthCore Clinics


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Sarah — appreciate the clarity here. High-level answers and proposed next steps below; attaching a short options brief (redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf) and a questionnaire template you can complete.

Short answers:
- Tenant-scoped retention: yes — per-tenant (org) policy with route-level overrides.
- Configurable windows: yes — arbitrary TTLs per field group (PHI, PII metadata) and per model/route.
- Irreversible redaction: we support irreversible field redaction (crypto-shredding of keys + deletion of token mappings) and a confirmable deletion workflow.
- Field-level options: both — we support ingest-time pseudonymization (hashing, reversible tokenization with KMS) and post-ingest deletion/redaction jobs. Ingest-time reduces footprint and risk but changes downstream analytics.
- Attachments: blobs are supported and can be deleted/zeroed out per schedule; we also offer OCR-based field extraction to allow selective redaction of text inside PDFs (additional processing step).

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Irreversible deletion for encrypted token mappings requires KMS key destruction or secure key-wrapping workflows; that has implications for legal holds and recoverability.
- Proof-of-deletion: we can export signed deletion manifests (timestamped, request-id, checksum-based) and delivery via secure S3 link or push to your SIEM.
- Availability: running aggressive background deletion/redaction jobs at scale can create IO spikes; we can schedule phased canary rollouts and rate-limit background work to preserve SLOs.

Next steps:
1) Can you share the high-level retention windows you expect for each data class (clinical notes, identifiers, attachments)?
2) If you prefer ingest-time pseudonymization vs. post-hoc deletion, tell us which workflows you want for the pilot.
3) We can schedule a 30-min tech call with Diego and Marcus (SRE/product) to walk process and show the deletion manifest format.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Notes on legal / compliance implications for HealthCore ask

Avery / Diego / Marcus — quick internal alignment before we reply with a formal proposal. Key points from legal/compliance perspective: 

1) Retention granularity: support per-tenant and per-route TTLs. We should propose default templates: 
   - PHI (identifiers, clinical notes): 90 days (pilot), configurable up to client-requested maximum;
   - PII-metadata (session IDs, usage logs w/o content): 365 days;
   - Attachments: configurable, default 180 days.

2) Redaction semantics: recommend offering two primary modes to customer: 
   - Ingest-time irreversible redaction: redact fields before they are stored; no keys retained. Use when client needs minimal retention risk.
   - Tokenize-with-reversible-keys: store tokens and KMS-wrapped mappings for cases where client requires later re-identification for support. Provide clear UI toggle and flag for legal_holds.

3) Proof-of-deletion: require support for signed manifests including: deletion_request_id, deleted_object_ids, checksums before/after, deletion_timestamp, operator_id, and canonical audit_hash. We can produce a PKI-signed manifest (service key) that HealthCore can verify. This satisfies auditors.

4) Audit / legal hold: note that destroying KMS keys is irreversible — if HealthCore needs retention for audit (some states require 6–7 years), advise tokenization instead of key destruction. We should surface the option to place objects into a restricted-hold bucket instead of destroying immediately.

5) SLA/Costs: background redaction at scale should be scoped as part of Dedicated / Private deployments. For hosted pilots, rate-limited deletion jobs and a small engineering change reduce risk. We should be transparent about CPU/GPU costs for OCR-based redaction of attachments.

Action: Marcus, can you draft the deletion-manifest schema and a sample export? Diego, please confirm S3/Syslog delivery options and any rate limits we should propose. Avery, I'll craft the compliance language for the proposal.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Thanks — that level of detail helps. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Some of our records (state-level requirements) require a minimum retention window of 7 years for certain records. Can the per-field policy be set to a minimum (e.g., 7 years) if we request that?
- We will likely want ingest-time tokenization for identifiers but keep clinical notes for 90 days then delete. Is Redwood able to support hybrid policies per-field in the same record?
- For proof-of-deletion, we prefer a push to our SIEM (Syslog/TLS) and an encrypted S3 drop for manifests — can you confirm both delivery methods exist?

I've attached the initial completed questionnaire (healthcore-data-retention-questionnaire.xlsx). If helpful, we can take the tech call Thursday morning PT.

— Sarah


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>, HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Sarah — great questions. Quick summary and proposed plan: 

- Minimum / Maximum windows: Yes — per-tenant fields can be configured to any minimum you require, including multi-year windows (we'll note the 7-year option in the proposal).
- Hybrid per-record policies: Supported. You can tokenize identifiers at ingest and set separate TTLs for clinical notes vs. token mappings. Token mappings can be set to retain for the required audit period or destroyed earlier depending on your preference.
- Delivery of deletion proofs: We support both Syslog/TLS push to a customer endpoint and encrypted S3 manifests. We'll include sample manifest payload and PKI signature during the tech call.

Proposed next steps:

text:
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Avery / Diego,

Thanks for taking this. HealthCore is evaluating Redwood for a pilot that will ingest clinical chat transcripts, structured vitals, and small document attachments that include PHI. Procurement asked me to capture a few specific requirements before we move to a security review: 

- Can Redwood support per-tenant retention policies (we need different windows per environment)?
- For PHI fields (patient name, MRN, SSN, clinical notes) we need a configurable maximum retention window and an automatic irreversible redaction option once the window expires.
- We require proof-of-deletion exports (auditable report) that we can store off-site for our auditors.
- Are there field-level redaction options (mask tokens / hashed pseudonymization) at ingest vs. deletion-time?
- Any limitations for attachments (PDFs/images) — can they also be redacted/removed on a per-tenant schedule?

We can share a filled questionnaire if that helps. Goal is to understand constraints (latency, costs, and whether this impacts availability SLAs).

Thanks — let me know if a quick call this week helps.

— Sarah
Sarah Kim
Director, Security & Compliance
HealthCore Clinics


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Sarah — appreciate the clarity here. High-level answers and proposed next steps below; attaching a short options brief (redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf) and a questionnaire template you can complete.

Short answers:
- Tenant-scoped retention: yes — per-tenant (org) policy with route-level overrides.
- Configurable windows: yes — arbitrary TTLs per field group (PHI, PII metadata) and per model/route.
- Irreversible redaction: we support irreversible field redaction (crypto-shredding of keys + deletion of token mappings) and a confirmable deletion workflow.
- Field-level options: both — we support ingest-time pseudonymization (hashing, reversible tokenization with KMS) and post-ingest deletion/redaction jobs. Ingest-time reduces footprint and risk but changes downstream analytics.
- Attachments: blobs are supported and can be deleted/zeroed out per schedule; we also offer OCR-based field extraction to allow selective redaction of text inside PDFs (additional processing step).

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Irreversible deletion for encrypted token mappings requires KMS key destruction or secure key-wrapping workflows; that has implications for legal holds and recoverability.
- Proof-of-deletion: we can export signed deletion manifests (timestamped, request-id, checksum-based) and delivery via secure S3 link or push to your SIEM.
- Availability: running aggressive background deletion/redaction jobs at scale can create IO spikes; we can schedule phased canary rollouts and rate-limit background work to preserve SLOs.

Next steps:
1) Can you share the high-level retention windows you expect for each data class (clinical notes, identifiers, attachments)?
2) If you prefer ingest-time pseudonymization vs. post-hoc deletion, tell us which workflows you want for the pilot.
3) We can schedule a 30-min tech call with Diego and Marcus (SRE/product) to walk process and show the deletion manifest format.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Notes on legal / compliance implications for HealthCore ask

Avery / Diego / Marcus — quick internal alignment before we reply with a formal proposal. Key points from legal/compliance perspective: 

1) Retention granularity: support per-tenant and per-route TTLs. We should propose default templates: 
   - PHI (identifiers, clinical notes): 90 days (pilot), configurable up to client-requested maximum;
   - PII-metadata (session IDs, usage logs w/o content): 365 days;
   - Attachments: configurable, default 180 days.

2) Redaction semantics: recommend offering two primary modes to customer: 
   - Ingest-time irreversible redaction: redact fields before they are stored; no keys retained. Use when client needs minimal retention risk.
   - Tokenize-with-reversible-keys: store tokens and KMS-wrapped mappings for cases where client requires later re-identification for support. Provide clear UI toggle and flag for legal_holds.

3) Proof-of-deletion: require support for signed manifests including: deletion_request_id, deleted_object_ids, checksums before/after, deletion_timestamp, operator_id, and canonical audit_hash. We can produce a PKI-signed manifest (service key) that HealthCore can verify. This satisfies auditors.

4) Audit / legal hold: note that destroying KMS keys is irreversible — if HealthCore needs retention for audit (some states require 6–7 years), advise tokenization instead of key destruction. We should surface the option to place objects into a restricted-hold bucket instead of destroying immediately.

5) SLA/Costs: background redaction at scale should be scoped as part of Dedicated / Private deployments. For hosted pilots, rate-limited deletion jobs and a small engineering change reduce risk. We should be transparent about CPU/GPU costs for OCR-based redaction of attachments.

Action: Marcus, can you draft the deletion-manifest schema and a sample export? Diego, please confirm S3/Syslog delivery options and any rate limits we should propose. Avery, I'll craft the compliance language for the proposal.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

- For proof-of-deletion, we prefer a push to our SIEM (Syslog/TLS) and an encrypted S3 drop for manifests — can you confirm both delivery methods exist?

Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Sarah — great questions. Quick summary and proposed plan: 

- Minimum / Maximum windows: Yes — per-tenant fields can be configured to any minimum you require, including multi-year windows (we'll note the 7-year option in the proposal).
- Hybrid per-record policies: Supported. You can tokenize identifiers at ingest and set separate TTLs for clinical notes vs. token mappings. Token mappings can be set to retain for the required audit period or destroyed earlier depending on your preference.
- Delivery of deletion proofs: We support both Syslog/TLS push to a customer endpoint and encrypted S3 manifests. We'll include sample manifest payload and PKI signature during the tech call.
